cause: Ticks (Rhipicephalus, Ixodes species) attach to skin during walks in grass or wooded areas. They feed on blood and can transmit diseases like Ehrlichiosis and Lyme disease.
warning: If dog shows hind-leg weakness or wobbling after tick bite — rush to vet immediately. This is Tick Paralysis, a medical emergency.

medicines.name: Frontline Plus (Fipronil + S-Methoprene)
medicines.dose: 1 pipette applied to skin between shoulder blades monthly
medicines.note: Do not bathe dog 48 hours before or after application

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Tick & Flea Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Lather, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Use weekly during active infestation.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Antiseptic Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply to bite site after tick removal to prevent infection

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Oral Coat Conditioner (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Soothes inflamed skin and rebuilds skin barrier damaged by tick bites
supplements.dose: 1 pump on food daily

safety_warnings: Never burn, squeeze or twist a tick — causes it to inject bacteria into the wound
Use fine-tipped tweezers, grasp close to skin, pull upward steadily
Treat ALL pets in the house simultaneously

cause: Ctenocephalides canis fleas jump onto dogs from infested grass, carpets, or other animals. 95% of fleas live in the environment, not on the dog.
warning: A single female flea lays 50 eggs per day — treat the home environment simultaneously or infestation returns within weeks.

medicines.name: Frontline Plus (Fipronil + S-Methoprene)
medicines.dose: Monthly spot-on between shoulder blades
medicines.note: Kills adult fleas AND stops egg development

medicines.name: Comfortis (Spinosad) Oral Tablet
medicines.dose: Once monthly with a full meal
medicines.note: Can begin reducing flea burden rapidly after administration.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Tick & Flea Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Lather thoroughly, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Repeat weekly during infestation.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 Fish Oil)
supplements.benefit: Reduces flea-bite skin inflammation and relieves itching
supplements.dose: Daily with food during and after infestation

safety_warnings: Wash all dog bedding at 60°C and vacuum all carpets — fleas survive months in fabric
Treat all pets in the house at the same time
Do not use dog flea products on cats — permethrin is fatal to cats

cause: Self-trauma from scratching or licking (triggered by flea bites, allergies, or moisture) creates a warm moist wound that Staphylococcus bacteria rapidly colonize and spread.
warning: Hot spots double in size within hours if licking continues. Use an E-collar immediately.

medicines.name: Betamethasone + Gentamicin Spray (Gentabet)
medicines.dose: Spray 2-3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: For larger or infected hot spots — requires vet prescription

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Antiseptic Spray
topical_treatments.use: Clip fur around hot spot. Clean with this spray before any cream. Use 2x daily.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply thin layer on cleaned hot spot after antiseptic spray dries

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces chronic skin inflammation that triggers hot spots from allergies
supplements.dose: Daily with food as long-term prevention

safety_warnings: Do not use steroid sprays on deep, bleeding, or pus-filled wounds without veterinary guidance.
Do NOT cover the hot spot — it needs air to dry out and heal
Use E-collar immediately — licking spreads bacteria and doubles size within hours
Hot spot larger than a coin or spreading fast = see vet for oral antibiotics

cause: Bacteria (Staphylococcus, Pseudomonas) or yeast (Malassezia) overgrowth inside the ear canal triggered by trapped moisture, allergies, or ear mites. Common in floppy-eared breeds.
warning: Head tilting to one side + loss of balance = middle ear infection (Otitis Media). Emergency vet visit needed immediately.

medicines.name: Otomax Ear Drops (Gentamicin + Betamethasone + Clotrimazole)
medicines.dose: 4 drops in affected ear 2x daily for 7 days. Massage ear base after each dose.
medicines.note: Covers bacteria AND yeast in one product — most effective for mixed infections

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Epi-Otic Ear Cleanser
topical_treatments.use: Fill ear canal, massage 30 seconds, let dog shake, wipe outer ear with cotton. Use before drops.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces allergy-driven ear inflammation — the #1 root cause of recurring ear infections
supplements.dose: Daily with meals as long-term preventative

safety_warnings: Do not use medicated ear drops unless the eardrum has been evaluated by a veterinarian.
NEVER insert cotton swabs deep into the ear — can rupture eardrum
Dry ears thoroughly after every bath and swim — moisture is the #1 trigger
Never use human ear drops — they contain alcohol that burns and damages dog ear tissue

cause: Sarcoptes scabiei mites burrow into the outer skin layer causing intense allergic itch. Spreads through direct contact with infected dogs or contaminated bedding.
warning: Sarcoptic mange is contagious to humans. Wear gloves when handling the dog and wash hands thoroughly after contact.

medicines.name: Fluralaner (Bravecto) Chewable Tablet
medicines.dose: Single oral dose by weight with food. One dose = 12 weeks coverage.
medicines.note: Begins killing mites rapidly after administration.

medicines.name: Ivermectin Injection
medicines.dose: Given by vet subcutaneously, typically weekly for 4-6 weeks
medicines.note: NEVER use in Collies, Shelties — MDR1 gene causes fatal ivermectin toxicity in these breeds

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Wear gloves. Lather, leave 10 minutes, rinse. Weekly during treatment to remove mite crusts.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply to raw crusted skin areas after bathing to prevent secondary bacterial infection

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 + Vitamin E)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds damaged skin barrier, reduces inflammatory itch, supports coat regrowth
supplements.dose: Daily throughout treatment period and 1 month after recovery

safety_warnings: Zoonotic — contagious to humans. Wear gloves at all times.
Treat ALL pets in the household simultaneously
Wash all bedding in hot water 60°C+ and disinfect living area — mites survive 36 hours off the host
NEVER use ivermectin in Collies, Shelties, Australian Shepherds

cause: Microsporum canis fungus infects hair follicles, causing the characteristic expanding circular patches. Spreads through contact with infected animals, soil, or shared grooming tools.
warning: Ringworm is highly contagious to humans, especially children. Wear gloves when handling the dog.

medicines.name: Itraconazole (Itrafungol / Sporanox)
medicines.dose: 5 mg/kg once daily with food for 3 weeks minimum
medicines.note: Preferred over Griseofulvin. Safer in puppies. Takes 6-8 weeks minimum for full clearance.

topical_treatments.name: Nizoral Shampoo (Ketoconazole 2%)
topical_treatments.use: Lather entire body, leave 10 minutes, rinse well. Use twice weekly for 6-8 weeks.

topical_treatments.name: Clotrimazole Cream 1% (Canesten)
topical_treatments.use: Apply to patch and 1 cm beyond the ring border twice daily for 4-6 weeks

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7) Supplement
supplements.benefit: Promotes rapid hair follicle recovery and coat regrowth in bald patches after infection clears
supplements.dose: Once daily with food for minimum 6 weeks during and after treatment

safety_warnings: Use veterinary guidance in sensitive-skinned dogs and avoid contact with eyes.
Highly contagious to humans — wear gloves, wash hands after contact
Disinfect all brushes, bedding, collars with 1:10 bleach solution — spores survive months
Continue treatment 2 weeks AFTER lesions appear healed to prevent relapse
Isolate the dog from children and other pets during treatment

cause: Genetic overreaction to environmental allergens (pollen, dust mites, mold) or food proteins (chicken, beef, wheat). The skin barrier is defective allowing allergens to penetrate.
warning: Apoquel must not be used in dogs under 12 months or those with serious infections. Consult vet before starting any immunosuppressive therapy.

medicines.name: Oclacitinib (Apoquel) Tablet
medicines.dose: 0.4-0.6 mg/kg twice daily for 14 days, then once daily maintenance
medicines.note: Works within 4 hours.

medicines.name: Cytopoint (Lokivetmab) Injection
medicines.dose: Single injection by vet every 4-8 weeks
medicines.note: Biologic therapy, very safe, lasts up to 8 weeks

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Allermyl Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Leave on 10 minutes. 2x weekly during flares, once weekly for maintenance.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-dose Omega-3 (EPA + DHA)
supplements.benefit: Reduces skin inflammation at cellular level and rebuilds skin barrier — reduces Apoquel dose needed over time
supplements.dose: 180 mg EPA + 120 mg DHA per 5 kg body weight daily. Results in 4-6 weeks.

safety_warnings: Use cautiously in dogs with immune suppression, cancer history, or active infections.
NEVER use human antihistamines like Benadryl without vet dosing guidance
Food allergy trials must use strict hydrolyzed or novel protein diet for 12 full weeks to be valid
Apoquel should not be used in dogs under 12 months

cause: Canine Parvovirus 2 (CPV-2) destroys intestinal lining and bone marrow. Spread through infected feces. Survives in soil for years. Most fatal in unvaccinated puppies 6 weeks to 6 months old.
warning: THIS IS A LIFE-THREATENING EMERGENCY. Go to a vet immediately — every hour matters. Mortality without treatment is up to 91%. There is NO effective home treatment.

medicines.name: IV Fluid Therapy (Normal Saline / Ringer's Lactate)
medicines.dose: Continuous IV drip calculated by vet based on dehydration level
medicines.note: The single most important treatment. Cannot be replaced by oral fluids — the gut is destroyed and cannot absorb.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg IV or SC once daily at vet clinic
medicines.note: Controls vomiting and allows the gut to rest and begin healing

medicines.name: Ampicillin IV (Antibiotic — prevents sepsis)
medicines.dose: 22 mg/kg IV every 6-8 hours in hospital
medicines.note: Prevents secondary bacterial sepsis while immune system is suppressed

supplements.name: Purina Pro Plan Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut microbiome destroyed by Parvovirus. ONLY start AFTER vomiting stops and dog can eat.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet sprinkled on food daily for 4-6 weeks post-discharge

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to vet immediately. Do not wait.
Do NOT attempt oral fluids at home — vomiting gut cannot absorb them and you lose critical time
Vaccinate puppies at 6, 8, 12, and 16 weeks — this disease is entirely preventable
Parvovirus survives in soil 7 years — disinfect all surfaces with 1:30 bleach solution

cause: Bordetella bronchiseptica bacteria alone or combined with canine parainfluenza virus. Spreads through the air in enclosed spaces (kennels, dog parks, grooming salons).
warning: If dog develops fever, stops eating, or cough lasts more than 3 weeks — see vet immediately. May have progressed to pneumonia.

medicines.name: Doxycycline
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days with food
medicines.note: For confirmed bacterial cases. Give with food to prevent nausea.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Steam Inhalation (Home Therapy)
topical_treatments.use: Run hot shower, close bathroom door, sit with dog in steam for 10 minutes twice daily. Do NOT direct steam nozzle at dog.

supplements.name: Manuka Honey (Natural Throat Soother)
supplements.benefit: Soothes throat lining, mild antibacterial, reduces cough irritation
supplements.dose: 0.5 tsp small dog / 1 tsp large dog, 2x daily.Do not give honey to very young puppies or diabetic dogs without veterinary guidance.

supplements.name: Vitamin C Immune Booster
supplements.benefit: May provide mild supportive antioxidant benefits.
supplements.dose: 125 mg per 5 kg body weight daily during illness

safety_warnings: Isolate from other dogs for 2 weeks — kennel cough is airborne and extremely contagious
Use a harness instead of neck collar — collar pressure on trachea worsens coughing
Vaccinate annually with Bordetella vaccine for dogs that visit kennels or dog parks

cause: Dietary indiscretion (eating grass, garbage, sudden diet change), bacterial/viral infection, intestinal parasites, stress, or food intolerance.
warning: See vet immediately if: blood in vomit or stool, dog is a puppy, symptoms last more than 48 hours, or dog is severely lethargic.

medicines.name: Metronidazole (Flagyl)
medicines.dose: 10-15 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days with food
medicines.note: Covers Giardia and Clostridium. Also has gut anti-inflammatory effect.

medicines.name: Kaolin-Pectin OTC
medicines.dose: 1-2 ml/kg every 4-6 hours
medicines.note: Coats gut lining, absorbs toxins, reduces fluid loss from diarrhea

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores healthy gut bacteria. Shortens diarrhea episodes by up to 50%.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet sprinkled on food daily. Start as soon as vomiting stops.

supplements.name: ORS / Electral Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces sodium, potassium, glucose lost through vomiting and diarrhea. Prevents dangerous dehydration.
supplements.dose: Offer as drinking water during illness. Do NOT force-feed to actively vomiting dogs.

safety_warnings: Temporary diet modification may help mild cases. Puppies, elderly dogs, or severely ill dogs should not be fasted without veterinary advice., then bland diet: boiled chicken + plain rice
NEVER give Imodium (loperamide) to Collies or Shelties — causes fatal neurological toxicity
NEVER give human anti-diarrheal tablets containing bismuth subsalicylate (Pepto-Bismol) — toxic to dogs
Blood in vomit or stool = emergency vet visit

cause: Roundworms from contaminated soil or mother's milk. Tapeworms from ingesting infected fleas. Hookworms penetrate through skin or ingestion. Puppies commonly infected from mother.
warning: Roundworms can infect humans (Toxocariasis). Hookworm larvae can penetrate human skin. Wash hands thoroughly after handling dog feces.

medicines.name: Fenbendazole (Panacur)
medicines.dose: 50 mg/kg once daily for 3 consecutive days. Repeat in 2-3 weeks.
medicines.note: Covers roundworms, hookworms, whipworms, Giardia.Often used in puppies and certain pregnant dogs under veterinary guidance.

medicines.name: Praziquantel + Pyrantel (Drontal Plus)
medicines.dose: Single dose by weight. Repeat in 2-3 weeks.
medicines.note: Best single-tablet option covering tapeworms AND roundworms/hookworms together

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut bacteria disrupted by worm damage and deworming medication
supplements.dose: Daily with food for 2-4 weeks after deworming

safety_warnings: Deworm puppies at 2, 4, 6, 8 weeks then monthly to 6 months, then quarterly
Treat fleas to prevent tapeworm re-infection — fleas carry tapeworm larvae
Dispose of dog feces immediately to prevent soil contamination and re-infection
Pale gums + extreme weakness = heavy hookworm burden causing anemia — emergency vet

cause: Bacterial infection (most common), viral infection, allergies, foreign body in eye, or anatomical issues (entropion) in flat-faced breeds.
warning: Cloudy eye, sudden vision loss, or bulging eye = emergency vet immediately. May be Glaucoma or Uveitis.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops (Tobrex)
medicines.dose: 1-2 drops in affected eye 3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: Broad-spectrum antibiotic. Use separate cotton ball for each eye to prevent cross-infection.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye Flush 0.9%
topical_treatments.use: Tilt dog's head, gently flush eye 2-3 times daily. Wipe clean with soft cotton. Use before eye drops.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces ocular inflammation and supports tear film stability in allergic conjunctivitis
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Eye conditions can appear similar. Pain, squinting, cloudiness, or vision changes require urgent veterinary examination.
NEVER use human Visine or Systane — contain vasoconstrictors harmful to dogs
Use a separate cotton ball for each eye to prevent spreading infection
If no improvement in 3 days with OTC care — see vet for prescription drops

cause: Genetic malformation of the hip joint causing laxity and progressive arthritis. Worsened by rapid growth in puppies, obesity, and high-impact exercise during development.
warning: NEVER give human NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen, paracetamol) — they cause fatal gastrointestinal ulcers and kidney failure in dogs.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food. Never exceed dose.
medicines.note: Most commonly prescribed dog NSAID. Monitor kidney function with long-term use.

medicines.name: Gabapentin
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily. Often combined with Meloxicam.
medicines.note: Addresses the nerve pain component of chronic hip dysplasia

supplements.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM (Cosequin DS)
supplements.benefit: Glucosamine rebuilds cartilage. Chondroitin retains joint fluid. MSM reduces inflammation.May support joint comfort and mobility in some dogs.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks, then maintenance. Takes 4-8 weeks to see results.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-Dose Omega-3
supplements.benefit: EPA reduces inflammatory prostaglandins destroying the joint. Studies show 40% reduction in joint pain with high-dose Omega-3.
supplements.dose: High dose — discuss with vet for correct dose by weight

safety_warnings: Gabapentin May cause sedation or unsteady walking, especially when starting therapy.
NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — cause fatal kidney failure in dogs
Maintain lean body weight — every extra kg puts 3-4 kg force on already damaged joints
Avoid high-impact activities — opt for swimming and leash walks on soft surfaces

cause: Normal wear and tear with age causing cartilage breakdown. Worsened by previous injuries, obesity, or breed predisposition (Labs, Goldens, German Shepherds).
warning: Get an orthopedic memory foam bed — hard floors significantly worsen arthritis pain. Install ramps instead of letting the dog jump.

medicines.name: Carprofen (Rimadyl) NSAID
medicines.dose: 2.2 mg/kg twice daily or 4.4 mg/kg once daily with food
medicines.note: Commonly prescribed veterinary NSAID. Monitor liver enzymes every 6 months with long-term use.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress / Heating Pad (Home Therapy)
topical_treatments.use: Apply warm damp cloth to stiff joints for 10-15 minutes twice daily, especially in cold weather. Do NOT use hot — test on your wrist first.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM)
supplements.benefit: Most studied joint supplement. Slows cartilage destruction, lubricates joints, reduces pain. Safe for indefinite long-term use.
supplements.dose: Double dose for first 4-6 weeks (loading phase). Allow 6-8 weeks for full effects.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (High Dose EPA+DHA)
supplements.benefit: Reduces inflammatory mediators in joint fluid. Studies show Omega-3 reduces NSAID dose needed by up to 25%.
supplements.dose: High dose by weight — discuss with vet

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — cause kidney failure and death in dogs
Get an orthopedic foam bed — hard surfaces dramatically worsen arthritis pain
Use ramps for furniture access — jumping causes acute joint injury on top of chronic arthritis

cause: E. coli (most common) bacteria ascending from the urethra into the bladder. More common in females due to shorter urethra. Worsened by obesity, bladder stones, or diabetes.
warning: If dog CANNOT urinate at all despite straining = BLOCKED URETHRA. Life-threatening emergency — go to vet immediately.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Synulox / Clavamox)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-14 days with food
medicines.note: Commonly prescribed for uncomplicated bacterial UTIs.. Always complete the full course even if symptoms resolve.

supplements.name: Cranberry Extract (PAC — Proanthocyanidins)
supplements.benefit: Prevents E. coli bacteria from adhering to bladder wall. Significantly reduces UTI recurrence in females.
supplements.dose: Daily for prevention. Not a substitute for antibiotics in active infection.

supplements.name: D-Mannose Powder
supplements.benefit: Natural sugar that coats E. coli bacteria preventing bladder wall attachment. May help reduce bacterial adherence in some cases.
supplements.dose: Mix into drinking water daily. Safe long-term. Combine with cranberry for best effect.

safety_warnings: Urinary symptoms may have multiple causes and often require urine testing for confirmation.
Complete inability to urinate = EMERGENCY — blocked urethra is fatal within hours without treatment
Recurrent UTIs (3+ per year) need investigation for bladder stones or diabetes
Always provide fresh water — diluted urine flushes the bladder and prevents bacterial growth

cause: Plaque and tartar buildup from lack of dental hygiene. Bacteria form a biofilm that inflames gums, destroys ligaments, and eventually infects the jaw bone.
warning: Severe dental disease may contribute to systemic health problems over time. It is not just a mouth problem.

medicines.name: Clindamycin (post-dental extraction)
medicines.dose: 5.5 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days after dental procedure
medicines.note: Prescribed after tooth extraction to prevent jaw bone infection

topical_treatments.name: Virbac C.E.T. Enzymatic Dog Toothpaste
topical_treatments.use: Brush daily or minimum 3x per week. NEVER use human toothpaste — xylitol is toxic to dogs.

topical_treatments.name: Tropiclean Fresh Breath Water Additive
topical_treatments.use: Add 1 capful per 250ml of water daily. Odorless and tasteless — dogs accept easily.

supplements.name: Calcium + Vitamin D3 (Drools Absolute Calcium)
supplements.benefit: Supports healthy tooth enamel and jaw bone density, reducing tooth loss from periodontal disease
supplements.dose: As per body weight. Do NOT over-supplement calcium in large breed puppies.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human toothpaste — fluoride and xylitol are toxic to dogs
Professional dental cleaning under anesthesia is the ONLY way to remove subgingival tartar
Dogs over 3 years have 80% chance of periodontal disease — start dental care as a puppy

cause: High-fat meal, dietary indiscretion (garbage, table scraps), certain medications (steroids), or trauma triggers digestive enzyme leakage that damages surrounding pancreatic tissue.
warning: Prayer position (front down, rear up) + vomiting + abdominal pain = potential severe pancreatitis. Emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) + IV Fluids
medicines.dose: Cerenia 1 mg/kg IV or SC once daily at vet. IV fluids continuous.
medicines.note: Controls vomiting and allows the pancreas to rest. Nothing by mouth until vomiting stops.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut flora disrupted by pancreatitis and antibiotic treatment
supplements.dose: Begin only after vomiting stops and dog can eat small amounts

supplements.name: Pancreatic Digestive Enzyme Supplement (Viokase-V)
supplements.benefit: Replaces digestive enzymes when pancreas is damaged. Reduces workload on the inflamed pancreas.
supplements.dose: Mix into food 20-30 minutes before feeding. Critical for EPI (Exocrine Pancreatic Insufficiency).

safety_warnings: Pancreatitis can be FATAL in severe cases — do not treat at home
Once a dog has had pancreatitis, permanent low-fat diet (under 10% fat DM) is mandatory
NEVER feed table scraps, bones, or fatty foods to a dog with history of pancreatitis

cause: Destruction of insulin-producing beta cells (Type 1 — most common in dogs) or insulin resistance. More common in intact females and middle-aged to senior dogs.
warning: NEVER skip insulin injections — leads to diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), a life-threatening emergency. Watch for hypoglycemia: trembling, weakness, collapse — give corn syrup on gums and rush to vet.

medicines.name: Caninsulin (Porcine Insulin) 40 IU/ml
medicines.dose: Injected subcutaneously twice daily 12 hours apart. ALWAYS given after meals.
medicines.note: Starting dose set by vet. Requires glucose curve monitoring. Never skip meals after giving insulin.

supplements.name: Chromium Picolinate (Blood Sugar Support)
supplements.benefit: Enhances insulin sensitivity and helps regulate blood glucose. May reduce insulin dose requirements.
supplements.dose: 200 mcg daily for small dogs, 400 mcg for large dogs. Use alongside prescribed insulin — NOT instead of it.

safety_warnings: Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) supplements are TOXIC to dogs — cause hypoglycemia and liver failure. Never give.
Intact female dogs have 3x diabetes risk — neutering often improves insulin sensitivity
Consistent meal times and diet are critical — any change can destabilize blood glucose

cause: Aging (most common), previous kidney infections, long-term NSAID use, dehydration, leptospirosis, certain toxin ingestion, or immune-mediated disease.
warning: NEVER give NSAIDs (Meloxicam, Carprofen) to a dog with kidney disease — they reduce blood flow to kidneys and cause acute kidney failure.

medicines.name: Benazepril (ACE Inhibitor)
medicines.dose: 0.25-0.5 mg/kg once daily
medicines.note: Reduces pressure in kidney blood vessels, slowing progression of CKD

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg once daily
medicines.note: Controls uremic nausea and significantly improves appetite and quality of life

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (EPA+DHA — Renoprotective)
supplements.benefit: Reduces glomerular inflammation. Clinical studies show it slows CKD progression and improves survival time.
supplements.dose: 40 mg EPA+DHA per kg body weight daily. One of the most evidence-based supplements for CKD.

supplements.name: Aluminum Hydroxide (Phosphate Binder)
supplements.benefit: Binds dietary phosphorus in the gut before absorption. Reducing phosphorus is the most important dietary intervention in CKD.
supplements.dose: Titrated by vet based on blood phosphorus levels. Mix into food.

safety_warnings: Renal prescription diet is NOT optional — it is the most critical intervention after diagnosis
NEVER give NSAIDs to a dog with kidney disease — can trigger acute-on-chronic kidney failure
Home subcutaneous fluid therapy taught by vet can add months to years of quality life

cause: Toxin ingestion (xylitol, mushrooms, medications), infectious hepatitis, chronic copper accumulation (Bedlingtons, Dalmatians), portosystemic shunt, or long-term steroid use.
warning: ANY yellow color in eyes or skin = medical emergency. Head pressing against walls = hepatic encephalopathy — emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Ursodeoxycholic Acid (UDCA / Ursodiol)
medicines.dose: 10-15 mg/kg once daily with food
medicines.note: Improves bile flow, reduces inflammation, protects liver cells. Safe for long-term use.

supplements.name: SAMe (S-Adenosyl Methionine) — Denosyl SD4
supplements.benefit: Most evidence-based liver supplement for dogs. Replenishes glutathione (liver's primary antioxidant), directly protecting liver cells.
supplements.dose: Give on empty stomach 1-2 hours before food. Do NOT crush the enteric coating.

supplements.name: Milk Thistle (Silymarin 70%) / Himalaya Liv.52 Vet
supplements.benefit: Silymarin protects liver cell membranes, stimulates regeneration, and has antifibrotic properties.
supplements.dose: 20-50 mg Silymarin per kg body weight daily. Use standardized 70-80% Silymarin extract.

safety_warnings: Yellow eyes or skin = EMERGENCY — see vet immediately
Head pressing against walls = hepatic encephalopathy = EMERGENCY
NEVER give Tylenol/paracetamol/acetaminophen to dogs — even one tablet causes fatal liver failure

cause: Lack of independence training in puppyhood, history of abandonment, trauma, or genetic predisposition to fear. Storm phobia often worsens with age.
warning: Punishment during anxiety attacks makes anxiety dramatically worse. Never scold an anxious dog.

medicines.name: Fluoxetine (Reconcile) SSRI
medicines.dose: 1-2 mg/kg once daily. Takes 4-6 weeks to reach full effect.
medicines.note: Must be combined with behavioral modification to be effective. Not a standalone fix.

medicines.name: Trazodone (Situational anxiety)
medicines.dose: 2-5 mg/kg every 6-8 hours as needed before known triggers
medicines.note: Works within 1-2 hours. Good for storms, fireworks, vet visits.

topical_treatments.name: Adaptil DAP Diffuser + Collar
topical_treatments.use: Plug diffuser into main room. Replace refill monthly. Collar worn continuously.

supplements.name: L-Theanine + Melatonin Calming Chew (Zesty Paws Calming Bites)
supplements.benefit: L-Theanine raises GABA and serotonin producing calm without sedation. Melatonin reduces physiological stress response.
supplements.dose: Give 30-60 minutes before trigger. Can be used daily for chronic anxiety.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human CBD products — many contain xylitol (toxic) or untested concentrations
Punishment worsens anxiety — never scold or punish an anxious dog
Meclizine (OTC motion sickness tablet) is safe at 25 mg/dose for vestibular or travel anxiety — ask vet first

cause: Staphylococcus pseudintermedius overgrowth following skin trauma, moisture, allergies (most common root cause), hormonal imbalance, or immune suppression.
warning: Recurrent pyoderma almost always has an underlying cause (allergies, hypothyroidism, Cushing's). Treating only the bacteria without addressing root cause leads to relapse.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Clavamox / Synulox)
medicines.dose: 12.5-25 mg/kg twice daily with food for 3-8 weeks
medicines.note: Complete the FULL course — stopping early causes antibiotic-resistant bacteria (MRSP)

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Pyohex Shampoo (Chlorhexidine 3% + Miconazole 1%)
topical_treatments.use: Leave on 5-10 minutes before rinsing. Use twice weekly throughout antibiotic course.

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Spray
topical_treatments.use: Spray on affected areas, allow to dry. Use daily between shampoo days.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 — Skin Barrier Repair)
supplements.benefit: Repairs the defective skin barrier that allowed bacterial overgrowth. Reduces allergy-driven inflammation triggering recurrent pyoderma.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Never stop antibiotics early even if skin looks better — biofilms need the full course to eradicate
MRSP (Methicillin-Resistant Staph) is emerging in dogs — culture before treating recurrent non-responding cases
Recurrent pyoderma without clear cause needs full hormonal and allergy workup

cause: Idiopathic epilepsy (genetic — Beagles, Labs, German Shepherds most common). Secondary epilepsy from brain tumor, head trauma, toxin ingestion, hypoglycemia, or liver disease.
warning: Seizure lasting more than 5 minutes OR multiple seizures within 1 hour = STATUS EPILEPTICUS — rush to emergency vet immediately. This is fatal without treatment.

medicines.name: Phenobarbital
medicines.dose: 2-3 mg/kg twice daily. Serum levels tested 2 weeks after starting.
medicines.note: Most effective first-line treatment. Requires liver enzyme monitoring every 6 months.

medicines.name: Potassium Bromide (KBr)
medicines.dose: 30-40 mg/kg once daily mixed into food
medicines.note: Added to Phenobarbital for better control. Takes 3-4 months to reach steady-state levels.

supplements.name: MCT Oil (Medium Chain Triglycerides)
supplements.benefit: Provides alternative fuel for neurons, reducing seizure frequency. Studies show up to 50% seizure reduction as adjunct to medication.
supplements.dose: Start 0.5 ml/kg daily, increase over 2-4 weeks. Too much causes diarrhea.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (DHA — Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: DHA is a primary structural component of brain cell membranes. Supports neurological health and may reduce seizure severity.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: During seizure: stay calm, time it, do NOT put anything in dog's mouth — they cannot swallow their tongue
NEVER stop anticonvulsant medication suddenly — causes severe rebound seizures that can be fatal
5+ minute seizure = emergency vet immediately

aliases: dog not putting weight on leg
cause: Overstretching or tearing of ligaments (sprain) or muscles/tendons (strain) from sudden movements, jumping, or rough play. Common in active and sporting breeds.
warning: NEVER give human painkillers (ibuprofen, paracetamol, aspirin, Crocin) — all are toxic and potentially fatal to dogs. Only use dog-specific NSAIDs prescribed by a vet.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) — Dog-safe NSAID only
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food for 5-7 days
medicines.note: ONLY safe painkiller for dogs. Never give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — these are fatal.

topical_treatments.name: Cold Pack Therapy (First 24-48 hours)
topical_treatments.use: Wrap ice pack in towel, apply to swollen area 10-15 minutes 3x daily for first 48 hours. Never apply ice directly to skin.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress (After 48 hours)
topical_treatments.use: After first 48 hours, switch to warm damp cloth for 10-15 minutes 2x daily to improve circulation and healing.

supplements.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin (Cosequin DS)
supplements.benefit: Supports ligament and cartilage repair during recovery. Reduces secondary joint inflammation.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4 weeks during recovery

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces acute joint and soft tissue inflammation during injury recovery
supplements.dose: Daily with food during recovery period

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, paracetamol, or Crocin — ALL are toxic to dogs and can cause fatal kidney failure
Strict rest for minimum 2 weeks — no running, jumping, or stairs
If dog is completely non-weight-bearing for more than 24 hours — see vet immediately to rule out fracture or ligament rupture

cause: Being left in a hot car, overexertion in hot humid weather, lack of shade/water. Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, French Bulldogs) cannot pant efficiently and overheat faster.
warning: NEVER use ice or ice water to cool a heatstroke dog — causes blood vessel spasm that traps heat inside the core and can worsen outcome.

medicines.name: IV Normal Saline (Emergency Internal Cooling + Shock)
medicines.dose: IV fluid resuscitation at vet clinic to cool internally and reverse shock
medicines.note: Most effective cooling method — more efficient than external cooling alone

topical_treatments.name: Tepid Water Cooling Protocol (Emergency First Aid)
topical_treatments.use: Apply room-temperature (NOT cold/ice) water to armpits, groin, paws. Fan continuously. Stop cooling when temperature reaches 39.5°C to prevent rebound hypothermia.

supplements.name: Electral / ORS Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces electrolytes lost through extreme panting and hyperthermia. Supports kidney recovery post-heatstroke.
supplements.dose: Offer freely once conscious and swallowing. Do NOT give to unconscious dog. Follow with IV fluids at vet.

safety_warnings: NEVER leave a dog in a parked car even for 5 minutes — temperature reaches fatal levels in under 10 minutes
NEVER use ice or ice water — causes peripheral vasoconstriction trapping heat in core
Frantic panting + bright red gums + collapse = heatstroke emergency. Rush to vet immediately.
Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, Frenchies) are at extreme risk even in mild heat

cause: Ticks (Rhipicephalus, Ixodes species) attach to skin during walks in grass or wooded areas. They feed on blood and can transmit diseases like Ehrlichiosis and Lyme disease.
warning: If dog shows hind-leg weakness or wobbling after tick bite — rush to vet immediately. This is Tick Paralysis, a medical emergency.

medicines.name: Frontline Plus (Fipronil + S-Methoprene)
medicines.dose: 1 pipette applied to skin between shoulder blades monthly
medicines.note: Do not bathe dog 48 hours before or after application

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Tick & Flea Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Lather, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Use weekly during active infestation.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Antiseptic Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply to bite site after tick removal to prevent infection

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Oral Coat Conditioner (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Soothes inflamed skin and rebuilds skin barrier damaged by tick bites
supplements.dose: 1 pump on food daily

safety_warnings: Never burn, squeeze or twist a tick — causes it to inject bacteria into the wound
Use fine-tipped tweezers, grasp close to skin, pull upward steadily
Treat ALL pets in the house simultaneously

cause: Ctenocephalides canis fleas jump onto dogs from infested grass, carpets, or other animals. 95% of fleas live in the environment, not on the dog.
warning: A single female flea lays 50 eggs per day — treat the home environment simultaneously or infestation returns within weeks.

medicines.name: Frontline Plus (Fipronil + S-Methoprene)
medicines.dose: Monthly spot-on between shoulder blades
medicines.note: Kills adult fleas AND stops egg development

medicines.name: Comfortis (Spinosad) Oral Tablet
medicines.dose: Once monthly with a full meal
medicines.note: Starts killing fleas within 30 minutes — faster than spot-ons

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Tick & Flea Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Lather thoroughly, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Repeat weekly during infestation.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 Fish Oil)
supplements.benefit: Reduces flea-bite skin inflammation and relieves itching
supplements.dose: Daily with food during and after infestation

safety_warnings: Wash all dog bedding at 60°C and vacuum all carpets — fleas survive months in fabric
Treat all pets in the house at the same time
Do not use dog flea products on cats — permethrin is fatal to cats

cause: Self-trauma from scratching or licking (triggered by flea bites, allergies, or moisture) creates a warm moist wound that Staphylococcus bacteria rapidly colonize and spread.
warning: Hot spots double in size within hours if licking continues. Use an E-collar immediately.

medicines.name: Hydrocortisone Spray 1%
medicines.dose: Spray thin layer on hot spot 2x daily for up to 7 days
medicines.note: Breaks the itch-scratch cycle. Do not use more than 7 days without vet guidance.

medicines.name: Betamethasone + Gentamicin Spray (Gentabet)
medicines.dose: Spray 2-3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: For larger or infected hot spots — requires vet prescription

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Antiseptic Spray
topical_treatments.use: Clip fur around hot spot. Clean with this spray before any cream. Use 2x daily.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply thin layer on cleaned hot spot after antiseptic spray dries

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces chronic skin inflammation that triggers hot spots from allergies
supplements.dose: Daily with food as long-term prevention

safety_warnings: Do NOT cover the hot spot — it needs air to dry out and heal
Use E-collar immediately — licking spreads bacteria and doubles size within hours
Hot spot larger than a coin or spreading fast = see vet for oral antibiotics

cause: Bacteria (Staphylococcus, Pseudomonas) or yeast (Malassezia) overgrowth inside the ear canal triggered by trapped moisture, allergies, or ear mites. Common in floppy-eared breeds.
warning: Head tilting to one side + loss of balance = middle ear infection (Otitis Media). Emergency vet visit needed immediately.

medicines.name: Otomax Ear Drops (Gentamicin + Betamethasone + Clotrimazole)
medicines.dose: 4 drops in affected ear 2x daily for 7 days. Massage ear base after each dose.
medicines.note: Covers bacteria AND yeast in one product — most effective for mixed infections

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Epi-Otic Ear Cleanser
topical_treatments.use: Fill ear canal, massage 30 seconds, let dog shake, wipe outer ear with cotton. Use before drops.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces allergy-driven ear inflammation — the #1 root cause of recurring ear infections
supplements.dose: Daily with meals as long-term preventative

safety_warnings: NEVER insert cotton swabs deep into the ear — can rupture eardrum
Dry ears thoroughly after every bath and swim — moisture is the #1 trigger
Never use human ear drops — they contain alcohol that burns and damages dog ear tissue

cause: Sarcoptes scabiei mites burrow into the outer skin layer causing intense allergic itch. Spreads through direct contact with infected dogs or contaminated bedding.
warning: Sarcoptic mange is contagious to humans. Wear gloves when handling the dog and wash hands thoroughly after contact.

medicines.name: Fluralaner (Bravecto) Chewable Tablet
medicines.dose: Single oral dose by weight with food. One dose = 12 weeks coverage.
medicines.note: Kills Sarcoptes mites within 24 hours. Most effective modern treatment.

medicines.name: Ivermectin Injection
medicines.dose: Given by vet subcutaneously, typically weekly for 4-6 weeks
medicines.note: NEVER use in Collies, Shelties — MDR1 gene causes fatal ivermectin toxicity in these breeds

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Wear gloves. Lather, leave 10 minutes, rinse. Weekly during treatment to remove mite crusts.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply to raw crusted skin areas after bathing to prevent secondary bacterial infection

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 + Vitamin E)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds damaged skin barrier, reduces inflammatory itch, supports coat regrowth
supplements.dose: Daily throughout treatment period and 1 month after recovery

safety_warnings: Zoonotic — contagious to humans. Wear gloves at all times.
Treat ALL pets in the household simultaneously
Wash all bedding in hot water 60°C+ and disinfect living area — mites survive 36 hours off the host
NEVER use ivermectin in Collies, Shelties, Australian Shepherds

cause: Microsporum canis fungus infects hair follicles, causing the characteristic expanding circular patches. Spreads through contact with infected animals, soil, or shared grooming tools.
warning: Ringworm is highly contagious to humans, especially children. Wear gloves when handling the dog.

medicines.name: Itraconazole (Itrafungol / Sporanox)
medicines.dose: 5 mg/kg once daily with food for 3 weeks minimum
medicines.note: Preferred over Griseofulvin. Safer in puppies. Takes 6-8 weeks minimum for full clearance.

topical_treatments.name: Nizoral Shampoo (Ketoconazole 2%)
topical_treatments.use: Lather entire body, leave 10 minutes, rinse well. Use twice weekly for 6-8 weeks.

topical_treatments.name: Clotrimazole Cream 1% (Canesten)
topical_treatments.use: Apply to patch and 1 cm beyond the ring border twice daily for 4-6 weeks

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7) Supplement
supplements.benefit: Promotes rapid hair follicle recovery and coat regrowth in bald patches after infection clears
supplements.dose: Once daily with food for minimum 6 weeks during and after treatment

safety_warnings: Highly contagious to humans — wear gloves, wash hands after contact
Disinfect all brushes, bedding, collars with 1:10 bleach solution — spores survive months
Continue treatment 2 weeks AFTER lesions appear healed to prevent relapse
Isolate the dog from children and other pets during treatment

cause: Genetic overreaction to environmental allergens (pollen, dust mites, mold) or food proteins (chicken, beef, wheat). The skin barrier is defective allowing allergens to penetrate.
warning: Apoquel must not be used in dogs under 12 months or those with serious infections. Consult vet before starting any immunosuppressive therapy.

medicines.name: Oclacitinib (Apoquel) Tablet
medicines.dose: 0.4-0.6 mg/kg twice daily for 14 days, then once daily maintenance
medicines.note: Works within 4 hours. Gold standard for atopic dermatitis. Prescription required.

medicines.name: Cytopoint (Lokivetmab) Injection
medicines.dose: Single injection by vet every 4-8 weeks
medicines.note: Biologic therapy, very safe, lasts up to 8 weeks

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Allermyl Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Leave on 10 minutes. 2x weekly during flares, once weekly for maintenance.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-dose Omega-3 (EPA + DHA)
supplements.benefit: Reduces skin inflammation at cellular level and rebuilds skin barrier — reduces Apoquel dose needed over time
supplements.dose: 180 mg EPA + 120 mg DHA per 5 kg body weight daily. Results in 4-6 weeks.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human antihistamines like Benadryl without vet dosing guidance
Food allergy trials must use strict hydrolyzed or novel protein diet for 12 full weeks to be valid
Apoquel should not be used in dogs under 12 months

cause: Canine Parvovirus 2 (CPV-2) destroys intestinal lining and bone marrow. Spread through infected feces. Survives in soil for years. Most fatal in unvaccinated puppies 6 weeks to 6 months old.
warning: THIS IS A LIFE-THREATENING EMERGENCY. Go to a vet immediately — every hour matters. Mortality without treatment is up to 91%. There is NO effective home treatment.

medicines.name: IV Fluid Therapy (Normal Saline / Ringer's Lactate)
medicines.dose: Continuous IV drip calculated by vet based on dehydration level
medicines.note: The single most important treatment. Cannot be replaced by oral fluids — the gut is destroyed and cannot absorb.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg IV or SC once daily at vet clinic
medicines.note: Controls vomiting and allows the gut to rest and begin healing

medicines.name: Ampicillin IV (Antibiotic — prevents sepsis)
medicines.dose: 22 mg/kg IV every 6-8 hours in hospital
medicines.note: Prevents secondary bacterial sepsis while immune system is suppressed

supplements.name: Purina Pro Plan Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut microbiome destroyed by Parvovirus. ONLY start AFTER vomiting stops and dog can eat.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet sprinkled on food daily for 4-6 weeks post-discharge

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to vet immediately. Do not wait.
Do NOT attempt oral fluids at home — vomiting gut cannot absorb them and you lose critical time
Vaccinate puppies at 6, 8, 12, and 16 weeks — this disease is entirely preventable
Parvovirus survives in soil 7 years — disinfect all surfaces with 1:30 bleach solution

cause: Bordetella bronchiseptica bacteria alone or combined with canine parainfluenza virus. Spreads through the air in enclosed spaces (kennels, dog parks, grooming salons).
warning: If dog develops fever, stops eating, or cough lasts more than 3 weeks — see vet immediately. May have progressed to pneumonia.

medicines.name: Doxycycline
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days with food
medicines.note: For confirmed bacterial cases. Give with food to prevent nausea.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Steam Inhalation (Home Therapy)
topical_treatments.use: Run hot shower, close bathroom door, sit with dog in steam for 10 minutes twice daily. Do NOT direct steam nozzle at dog.

supplements.name: Manuka Honey (Natural Throat Soother)
supplements.benefit: Soothes throat lining, mild antibacterial, reduces cough irritation
supplements.dose: 0.5 tsp small dog / 1 tsp large dog, 2x daily. Do NOT give to puppies under 1 year.

supplements.name: Vitamin C Immune Booster
supplements.benefit: Supports immune response and speeds recovery from respiratory infection
supplements.dose: 125 mg per 5 kg body weight daily during illness

safety_warnings: Isolate from other dogs for 2 weeks — kennel cough is airborne and extremely contagious
Use a harness instead of neck collar — collar pressure on trachea worsens coughing
Vaccinate annually with Bordetella vaccine for dogs that visit kennels or dog parks

cause: Dietary indiscretion (eating grass, garbage, sudden diet change), bacterial/viral infection, intestinal parasites, stress, or food intolerance.
warning: See vet immediately if: blood in vomit or stool, dog is a puppy, symptoms last more than 48 hours, or dog is severely lethargic.

medicines.name: Metronidazole (Flagyl)
medicines.dose: 10-15 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days with food
medicines.note: Covers Giardia and Clostridium. Also has gut anti-inflammatory effect.

medicines.name: Kaolin-Pectin OTC
medicines.dose: 1-2 ml/kg every 4-6 hours
medicines.note: Coats gut lining, absorbs toxins, reduces fluid loss from diarrhea

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores healthy gut bacteria. Shortens diarrhea episodes by up to 50%.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet sprinkled on food daily. Start as soon as vomiting stops.

supplements.name: ORS / Electral Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces sodium, potassium, glucose lost through vomiting and diarrhea. Prevents dangerous dehydration.
supplements.dose: Offer as drinking water during illness. Do NOT force-feed to actively vomiting dogs.

safety_warnings: Fast for 12-24 hours (water available), then bland diet: boiled chicken + plain rice
NEVER give Imodium (loperamide) to Collies or Shelties — causes fatal neurological toxicity
NEVER give human anti-diarrheal tablets containing bismuth subsalicylate (Pepto-Bismol) — toxic to dogs
Blood in vomit or stool = emergency vet visit

cause: Roundworms from contaminated soil or mother's milk. Tapeworms from ingesting infected fleas. Hookworms penetrate through skin or ingestion. Puppies commonly infected from mother.
warning: Roundworms can infect humans (Toxocariasis). Hookworm larvae can penetrate human skin. Wash hands thoroughly after handling dog feces.

medicines.name: Fenbendazole (Panacur)
medicines.dose: 50 mg/kg once daily for 3 consecutive days. Repeat in 2-3 weeks.
medicines.note: Covers roundworms, hookworms, whipworms, Giardia. Safe in puppies and pregnant dogs.

medicines.name: Praziquantel + Pyrantel (Drontal Plus)
medicines.dose: Single dose by weight. Repeat in 2-3 weeks.
medicines.note: Best single-tablet option covering tapeworms AND roundworms/hookworms together

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut bacteria disrupted by worm damage and deworming medication
supplements.dose: Daily with food for 2-4 weeks after deworming

safety_warnings: Deworm puppies at 2, 4, 6, 8 weeks then monthly to 6 months, then quarterly
Treat fleas to prevent tapeworm re-infection — fleas carry tapeworm larvae
Dispose of dog feces immediately to prevent soil contamination and re-infection
Pale gums + extreme weakness = heavy hookworm burden causing anemia — emergency vet

cause: Bacterial infection (most common), viral infection, allergies, foreign body in eye, or anatomical issues (entropion) in flat-faced breeds.
warning: Cloudy eye, sudden vision loss, or bulging eye = emergency vet immediately. May be Glaucoma or Uveitis.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops (Tobrex)
medicines.dose: 1-2 drops in affected eye 3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: Broad-spectrum antibiotic. Use separate cotton ball for each eye to prevent cross-infection.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye Flush 0.9%
topical_treatments.use: Tilt dog's head, gently flush eye 2-3 times daily. Wipe clean with soft cotton. Use before eye drops.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Reduces ocular inflammation and supports tear film stability in allergic conjunctivitis
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: NEVER use human Visine or Systane — contain vasoconstrictors harmful to dogs
Use a separate cotton ball for each eye to prevent spreading infection
If no improvement in 3 days with OTC care — see vet for prescription drops

cause: Genetic malformation of the hip joint causing laxity and progressive arthritis. Worsened by rapid growth in puppies, obesity, and high-impact exercise during development.
warning: NEVER give human NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen, paracetamol) — they cause fatal gastrointestinal ulcers and kidney failure in dogs.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food. Never exceed dose.
medicines.note: Most commonly prescribed dog NSAID. Monitor kidney function with long-term use.

medicines.name: Gabapentin
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily. Often combined with Meloxicam.
medicines.note: Addresses the nerve pain component of chronic hip dysplasia

supplements.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM (Cosequin DS)
supplements.benefit: Glucosamine rebuilds cartilage. Chondroitin retains joint fluid. MSM reduces inflammation. Clinically proven to slow progression.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks, then maintenance. Takes 4-8 weeks to see results.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-Dose Omega-3
supplements.benefit: EPA reduces inflammatory prostaglandins destroying the joint. Studies show 40% reduction in joint pain with high-dose Omega-3.
supplements.dose: High dose — discuss with vet for correct dose by weight

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — cause fatal kidney failure in dogs
Maintain lean body weight — every extra kg puts 3-4 kg force on already damaged joints
Avoid high-impact activities — opt for swimming and leash walks on soft surfaces

cause: Normal wear and tear with age causing cartilage breakdown. Worsened by previous injuries, obesity, or breed predisposition (Labs, Goldens, German Shepherds).
warning: Get an orthopedic memory foam bed — hard floors significantly worsen arthritis pain. Install ramps instead of letting the dog jump.

medicines.name: Carprofen (Rimadyl) NSAID
medicines.dose: 2.2 mg/kg twice daily or 4.4 mg/kg once daily with food
medicines.note: Gold standard for canine arthritis. Monitor liver enzymes every 6 months with long-term use.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress / Heating Pad (Home Therapy)
topical_treatments.use: Apply warm damp cloth to stiff joints for 10-15 minutes twice daily, especially in cold weather. Do NOT use hot — test on your wrist first.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM)
supplements.benefit: Most studied joint supplement. Slows cartilage destruction, lubricates joints, reduces pain. Safe for indefinite long-term use.
supplements.dose: Double dose for first 4-6 weeks (loading phase). Allow 6-8 weeks for full effects.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (High Dose EPA+DHA)
supplements.benefit: Reduces inflammatory mediators in joint fluid. Studies show Omega-3 reduces NSAID dose needed by up to 25%.
supplements.dose: High dose by weight — discuss with vet

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — cause kidney failure and death in dogs
Get an orthopedic foam bed — hard surfaces dramatically worsen arthritis pain
Use ramps for furniture access — jumping causes acute joint injury on top of chronic arthritis

cause: E. coli (most common) bacteria ascending from the urethra into the bladder. More common in females due to shorter urethra. Worsened by obesity, bladder stones, or diabetes.
warning: If dog CANNOT urinate at all despite straining = BLOCKED URETHRA. Life-threatening emergency — go to vet immediately.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Synulox / Clavamox)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-14 days with food
medicines.note: First-line for uncomplicated UTI. Always complete the full course even if symptoms resolve.

supplements.name: Cranberry Extract (PAC — Proanthocyanidins)
supplements.benefit: Prevents E. coli bacteria from adhering to bladder wall. Significantly reduces UTI recurrence in females.
supplements.dose: Daily for prevention. Not a substitute for antibiotics in active infection.

supplements.name: D-Mannose Powder
supplements.benefit: Natural sugar that coats E. coli bacteria preventing bladder wall attachment. Works at the infection site directly in urine.
supplements.dose: Mix into drinking water daily. Safe long-term. Combine with cranberry for best effect.

safety_warnings: Complete inability to urinate = EMERGENCY — blocked urethra is fatal within hours without treatment
Recurrent UTIs (3+ per year) need investigation for bladder stones or diabetes
Always provide fresh water — diluted urine flushes the bladder and prevents bacterial growth

cause: Plaque and tartar buildup from lack of dental hygiene. Bacteria form a biofilm that inflames gums, destroys ligaments, and eventually infects the jaw bone.
warning: Dental disease bacteria enter the bloodstream and damage the heart, kidneys, and liver. It is not just a mouth problem.

medicines.name: Clindamycin (post-dental extraction)
medicines.dose: 5.5 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days after dental procedure
medicines.note: Prescribed after tooth extraction to prevent jaw bone infection

topical_treatments.name: Virbac C.E.T. Enzymatic Dog Toothpaste
topical_treatments.use: Brush daily or minimum 3x per week. NEVER use human toothpaste — xylitol is toxic to dogs.

topical_treatments.name: Tropiclean Fresh Breath Water Additive
topical_treatments.use: Add 1 capful per 250ml of water daily. Odorless and tasteless — dogs accept easily.

supplements.name: Calcium + Vitamin D3 (Drools Absolute Calcium)
supplements.benefit: Supports healthy tooth enamel and jaw bone density, reducing tooth loss from periodontal disease
supplements.dose: As per body weight. Do NOT over-supplement calcium in large breed puppies.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human toothpaste — fluoride and xylitol are toxic to dogs
Professional dental cleaning under anesthesia is the ONLY way to remove subgingival tartar
Dogs over 3 years have 80% chance of periodontal disease — start dental care as a puppy

cause: High-fat meal, dietary indiscretion (garbage, table scraps), certain medications (steroids), or trauma triggers digestive enzyme leakage that damages surrounding pancreatic tissue.
warning: Prayer position (front down, rear up) + vomiting + abdominal pain = potential severe pancreatitis. Emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) + IV Fluids
medicines.dose: Cerenia 1 mg/kg IV or SC once daily at vet. IV fluids continuous.
medicines.note: Controls vomiting and allows the pancreas to rest. Nothing by mouth until vomiting stops.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut flora disrupted by pancreatitis and antibiotic treatment
supplements.dose: Begin only after vomiting stops and dog can eat small amounts

supplements.name: Pancreatic Digestive Enzyme Supplement (Viokase-V)
supplements.benefit: Replaces digestive enzymes when pancreas is damaged. Reduces workload on the inflamed pancreas.
supplements.dose: Mix into food 20-30 minutes before feeding. Critical for EPI (Exocrine Pancreatic Insufficiency).

safety_warnings: Pancreatitis can be FATAL in severe cases — do not treat at home
Once a dog has had pancreatitis, permanent low-fat diet (under 10% fat DM) is mandatory
NEVER feed table scraps, bones, or fatty foods to a dog with history of pancreatitis

cause: Destruction of insulin-producing beta cells (Type 1 — most common in dogs) or insulin resistance. More common in intact females and middle-aged to senior dogs.
warning: NEVER skip insulin injections — leads to diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), a life-threatening emergency. Watch for hypoglycemia: trembling, weakness, collapse — give corn syrup on gums and rush to vet.

medicines.name: Caninsulin (Porcine Insulin) 40 IU/ml
medicines.dose: Injected subcutaneously twice daily 12 hours apart. ALWAYS given after meals.
medicines.note: Starting dose set by vet. Requires glucose curve monitoring. Never skip meals after giving insulin.

supplements.name: Chromium Picolinate (Blood Sugar Support)
supplements.benefit: Enhances insulin sensitivity and helps regulate blood glucose. May reduce insulin dose requirements.
supplements.dose: 200 mcg daily for small dogs, 400 mcg for large dogs. Use alongside prescribed insulin — NOT instead of it.

safety_warnings: Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) supplements are TOXIC to dogs — cause hypoglycemia and liver failure. Never give.
Intact female dogs have 3x diabetes risk — neutering often improves insulin sensitivity
Consistent meal times and diet are critical — any change can destabilize blood glucose

cause: Aging (most common), previous kidney infections, long-term NSAID use, dehydration, leptospirosis, certain toxin ingestion, or immune-mediated disease.
warning: NEVER give NSAIDs (Meloxicam, Carprofen) to a dog with kidney disease — they reduce blood flow to kidneys and cause acute kidney failure.

medicines.name: Benazepril (ACE Inhibitor)
medicines.dose: 0.25-0.5 mg/kg once daily
medicines.note: Reduces pressure in kidney blood vessels, slowing progression of CKD

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg once daily
medicines.note: Controls uremic nausea and significantly improves appetite and quality of life

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (EPA+DHA — Renoprotective)
supplements.benefit: Reduces glomerular inflammation. Clinical studies show it slows CKD progression and improves survival time.
supplements.dose: 40 mg EPA+DHA per kg body weight daily. One of the most evidence-based supplements for CKD.

supplements.name: Aluminum Hydroxide (Phosphate Binder)
supplements.benefit: Binds dietary phosphorus in the gut before absorption. Reducing phosphorus is the most important dietary intervention in CKD.
supplements.dose: Titrated by vet based on blood phosphorus levels. Mix into food.

safety_warnings: Renal prescription diet is NOT optional — it is the most critical intervention after diagnosis
NEVER give NSAIDs to a dog with kidney disease — can trigger acute-on-chronic kidney failure
Home subcutaneous fluid therapy taught by vet can add months to years of quality life

cause: Toxin ingestion (xylitol, mushrooms, medications), infectious hepatitis, chronic copper accumulation (Bedlingtons, Dalmatians), portosystemic shunt, or long-term steroid use.
warning: ANY yellow color in eyes or skin = medical emergency. Head pressing against walls = hepatic encephalopathy — emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Ursodeoxycholic Acid (UDCA / Ursodiol)
medicines.dose: 10-15 mg/kg once daily with food
medicines.note: Improves bile flow, reduces inflammation, protects liver cells. Safe for long-term use.

supplements.name: SAMe (S-Adenosyl Methionine) — Denosyl SD4
supplements.benefit: Most evidence-based liver supplement for dogs. Replenishes glutathione (liver's primary antioxidant), directly protecting liver cells.
supplements.dose: Give on empty stomach 1-2 hours before food. Do NOT crush the enteric coating.

supplements.name: Milk Thistle (Silymarin 70%) / Himalaya Liv.52 Vet
supplements.benefit: Silymarin protects liver cell membranes, stimulates regeneration, and has antifibrotic properties.
supplements.dose: 20-50 mg Silymarin per kg body weight daily. Use standardized 70-80% Silymarin extract.

safety_warnings: Yellow eyes or skin = EMERGENCY — see vet immediately
Head pressing against walls = hepatic encephalopathy = EMERGENCY
NEVER give Tylenol/paracetamol/acetaminophen to dogs — even one tablet causes fatal liver failure

cause: Lack of independence training in puppyhood, history of abandonment, trauma, or genetic predisposition to fear. Storm phobia often worsens with age.
warning: Punishment during anxiety attacks makes anxiety dramatically worse. Never scold an anxious dog.

medicines.name: Fluoxetine (Reconcile) SSRI
medicines.dose: 1-2 mg/kg once daily. Takes 4-6 weeks to reach full effect.
medicines.note: Must be combined with behavioral modification to be effective. Not a standalone fix.

medicines.name: Trazodone (Situational anxiety)
medicines.dose: 2-5 mg/kg every 6-8 hours as needed before known triggers
medicines.note: Works within 1-2 hours. Good for storms, fireworks, vet visits.

topical_treatments.name: Adaptil DAP Diffuser + Collar
topical_treatments.use: Plug diffuser into main room. Replace refill monthly. Collar worn continuously.

supplements.name: L-Theanine + Melatonin Calming Chew (Zesty Paws Calming Bites)
supplements.benefit: L-Theanine raises GABA and serotonin producing calm without sedation. Melatonin reduces physiological stress response.
supplements.dose: Give 30-60 minutes before trigger. Can be used daily for chronic anxiety.

safety_warnings: NEVER use human CBD products — many contain xylitol (toxic) or untested concentrations
Punishment worsens anxiety — never scold or punish an anxious dog
Meclizine (OTC motion sickness tablet) is safe at 25 mg/dose for vestibular or travel anxiety — ask vet first

cause: Staphylococcus pseudintermedius overgrowth following skin trauma, moisture, allergies (most common root cause), hormonal imbalance, or immune suppression.
warning: Recurrent pyoderma almost always has an underlying cause (allergies, hypothyroidism, Cushing's). Treating only the bacteria without addressing root cause leads to relapse.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Clavamox / Synulox)
medicines.dose: 12.5-25 mg/kg twice daily with food for 3-8 weeks
medicines.note: Complete the FULL course — stopping early causes antibiotic-resistant bacteria (MRSP)

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Pyohex Shampoo (Chlorhexidine 3% + Miconazole 1%)
topical_treatments.use: Leave on 5-10 minutes before rinsing. Use twice weekly throughout antibiotic course.

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Spray
topical_treatments.use: Spray on affected areas, allow to dry. Use daily between shampoo days.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow (Omega-3 — Skin Barrier Repair)
supplements.benefit: Repairs the defective skin barrier that allowed bacterial overgrowth. Reduces allergy-driven inflammation triggering recurrent pyoderma.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Never stop antibiotics early even if skin looks better — biofilms need the full course to eradicate
MRSP (Methicillin-Resistant Staph) is emerging in dogs — culture before treating recurrent non-responding cases
Recurrent pyoderma without clear cause needs full hormonal and allergy workup

cause: Idiopathic epilepsy (genetic — Beagles, Labs, German Shepherds most common). Secondary epilepsy from brain tumor, head trauma, toxin ingestion, hypoglycemia, or liver disease.
warning: Seizure lasting more than 5 minutes OR multiple seizures within 1 hour = STATUS EPILEPTICUS — rush to emergency vet immediately. This is fatal without treatment.

medicines.name: Phenobarbital
medicines.dose: 2-3 mg/kg twice daily. Serum levels tested 2 weeks after starting.
medicines.note: Most effective first-line treatment. Requires liver enzyme monitoring every 6 months.

medicines.name: Potassium Bromide (KBr)
medicines.dose: 30-40 mg/kg once daily mixed into food
medicines.note: Added to Phenobarbital for better control. Takes 3-4 months to reach steady-state levels.

supplements.name: MCT Oil (Medium Chain Triglycerides)
supplements.benefit: Provides alternative fuel for neurons, reducing seizure frequency. Studies show up to 50% seizure reduction as adjunct to medication.
supplements.dose: Start 0.5 ml/kg daily, increase over 2-4 weeks. Too much causes diarrhea.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (DHA — Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: DHA is a primary structural component of brain cell membranes. Supports neurological health and may reduce seizure severity.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: During seizure: stay calm, time it, do NOT put anything in dog's mouth — they cannot swallow their tongue
NEVER stop anticonvulsant medication suddenly — causes severe rebound seizures that can be fatal
5+ minute seizure = emergency vet immediately

aliases: dog not putting weight on leg
cause: Overstretching or tearing of ligaments (sprain) or muscles/tendons (strain) from sudden movements, jumping, or rough play. Common in active and sporting breeds.
warning: NEVER give human painkillers (ibuprofen, paracetamol, aspirin, Crocin) — all are toxic and potentially fatal to dogs. Only use dog-specific NSAIDs prescribed by a vet.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) — Dog-safe NSAID only
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food for 5-7 days
medicines.note: ONLY safe painkiller for dogs. Never give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — these are fatal.

topical_treatments.name: Cold Pack Therapy (First 24-48 hours)
topical_treatments.use: Wrap ice pack in towel, apply to swollen area 10-15 minutes 3x daily for first 48 hours. Never apply ice directly to skin.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress (After 48 hours)
topical_treatments.use: After first 48 hours, switch to warm damp cloth for 10-15 minutes 2x daily to improve circulation and healing.

supplements.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin (Cosequin DS)
supplements.benefit: Supports ligament and cartilage repair during recovery. Reduces secondary joint inflammation.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4 weeks during recovery

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces acute joint and soft tissue inflammation during injury recovery
supplements.dose: Daily with food during recovery period

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, paracetamol, or Crocin — ALL are toxic to dogs and can cause fatal kidney failure
Strict rest for minimum 2 weeks — no running, jumping, or stairs
If dog is completely non-weight-bearing for more than 24 hours — see vet immediately to rule out fracture or ligament rupture

cause: Being left in a hot car, overexertion in hot humid weather, lack of shade/water. Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, French Bulldogs) cannot pant efficiently and overheat faster.
warning: NEVER use ice or ice water to cool a heatstroke dog — causes blood vessel spasm that traps heat inside the core and can worsen outcome.

medicines.name: IV Normal Saline (Emergency Internal Cooling + Shock)
medicines.dose: IV fluid resuscitation at vet clinic to cool internally and reverse shock
medicines.note: Most effective cooling method — more efficient than external cooling alone

topical_treatments.name: Tepid Water Cooling Protocol (Emergency First Aid)
topical_treatments.use: Apply room-temperature (NOT cold/ice) water to armpits, groin, paws. Fan continuously. Stop cooling when temperature reaches 39.5°C to prevent rebound hypothermia.

supplements.name: Electral / ORS Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces electrolytes lost through extreme panting and hyperthermia. Supports kidney recovery post-heatstroke.
supplements.dose: Offer freely once conscious and swallowing. Do NOT give to unconscious dog. Follow with IV fluids at vet.

safety_warnings: NEVER leave a dog in a parked car even for 5 minutes — temperature reaches fatal levels in under 10 minutes
NEVER use ice or ice water — causes peripheral vasoconstriction trapping heat in core
Frantic panting + bright red gums + collapse = heatstroke emergency. Rush to vet immediately.
Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, Frenchies) are at extreme risk even in mild heat

cause: Congenital malformation of the trochlear groove causing the kneecap to slip out of position. Genetic condition — very common in small breeds: Chihuahuas, Pomeranians, Yorkshire Terriers, Toy Poodles, Maltese.
warning: Grade 3-4 patellar luxation causes permanent bone deformity and severe arthritis without surgery. Do not delay surgical consultation.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food for acute pain flares only
medicines.note: Short-term pain relief during episodes. Not a long-term solution — surgery needed for Grade 3-4.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin)
supplements.benefit: Maintains trochlear groove cartilage health and reduces secondary arthritis from repeated luxation events
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks, then maintenance. Use daily continuously.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces synovial joint inflammation from repeated cartilage trauma during luxation
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Grade 3-4 luxation needs surgery — permanent arthritis and disability without it
Avoid jumping, stairs, and twisting movements — these worsen cartilage damage
Do not breed dogs with Grade 3-4 patellar luxation — it is an inheritable condition
NEVER give ibuprofen or paracetamol — use only vet-prescribed Meloxicam

aliases: dog non weight bearing hind leg
cause: Rupture of the cranial cruciate ligament from acute trauma (jumping awkwardly) or chronic degeneration over time. Common in Labrador Retrievers, Rottweilers, Staffordshire Bull Terriers, and overweight dogs.
warning: An unstable knee destroys the meniscus cartilage within weeks. Surgery delayed beyond 4-6 weeks causes irreversible joint damage. Without surgery in large dogs, the opposite leg often tears within 1-2 years.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food during peri-surgical period
medicines.note: NEVER give human NSAIDs — ibuprofen and paracetamol are fatal to dogs

topical_treatments.name: Cold Pack Therapy (First 48 hours post-injury)
topical_treatments.use: Wrap ice pack in towel, apply to knee 10-15 minutes 3x daily for first 48 hours only. Reduces acute swelling.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin + Hyaluronic Acid)
supplements.benefit: Prevents secondary meniscal and cartilage damage in the unstable joint. Essential before and after surgery.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose immediately on diagnosis. Continue daily indefinitely.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-Dose Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces synovitis (joint lining inflammation) driven by the unstable knee. Slows cartilage breakdown.
supplements.dose: High dose by body weight — discuss with vet

safety_warnings: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — all are fatal to dogs
TPLO surgery has 90%+ return to full function — the most successful treatment for large breeds
Strict rest — no running, jumping, or off-leash activity until surgically repaired
Without surgery, the dog will develop severe painful arthritis within months

cause: Disc material herniates and compresses the spinal cord. Genetic predisposition in Chondrodystrophic breeds: Dachshunds, Corgis, Basset Hounds, Beagles, French Bulldogs, Shih Tzus.
warning: ANY hind leg weakness in a Dachshund is an EMERGENCY. Spinal cord death is permanent after 24-48 hours of compression. Do not wait.

medicines.name: Gabapentin + Methocarbamol
medicines.dose: Gabapentin 5-10 mg/kg twice daily. Methocarbamol per vet dosing.
medicines.note: Controls severe spinal pain and muscle spasms. Used for conservative management cases.

medicines.name: Methylprednisolone IV (Emergency neuroprotection)
medicines.dose: 30 mg/kg IV bolus within 8 hours of injury onset
medicines.note: Must be given EARLY for neuroprotection. Only if surgery is not being performed.

supplements.name: Methylcobalamin B12 + B-Vitamin Complex
supplements.benefit: Essential for myelin sheath repair and nerve fiber regeneration after spinal cord compression
supplements.dose: Oral supplementation at home after acute phase stabilizes. IV B vitamins given at vet clinic during acute phase.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (DHA — Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: DHA is primary fat in myelin (nerve coating). High-dose supports nerve regeneration and reduces spinal cord inflammation.
supplements.dose: High dose during recovery — discuss with neurologist

safety_warnings: Hind leg weakness in Dachshund = EMERGENCY — go to vet immediately, every hour matters
Strict cage rest means NO jumping, stairs, or running — one small room only for 6-8 weeks
Physical rehabilitation after surgery dramatically improves recovery — find a vet rehab specialist
Never lift a dog with IVDD by the torso — support front and back equally

cause: Canine Distemper Virus (CDV) spread through airborne respiratory secretions. Attacks respiratory, gastrointestinal, and nervous systems. Most fatal in unvaccinated puppies and young dogs.
warning: THIS IS A MEDICAL EMERGENCY. Distemper is largely fatal or causes permanent neurological damage without early intensive treatment. Vaccinate with DHPPiL vaccine — entirely preventable.

medicines.name: IV Fluid Therapy + Broad-spectrum Antibiotics
medicines.dose: Continuous IV fluids, ampicillin IV, anti-nausea medication — full hospital protocol
medicines.note: No antiviral cure exists. Treatment is intensive supportive care only.

medicines.name: Phenobarbital (Anti-seizure)
medicines.dose: Per vet protocol for neurological distemper cases
medicines.note: Controls seizures but does not treat the virus. Supportive only.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye and Nose Wipes
topical_treatments.use: Gently wipe discharge from eyes and nose 3-4 times daily with soft cotton dampened in saline. Prevents secondary infection.

supplements.name: Beaphar Multivitamine Liquid (Vitamin A + C + E)
supplements.benefit: Antioxidant vitamins support immune function during recovery and protect against tissue damage from viral infection
supplements.dose: Only after stabilization under vet care. Not a substitute for treatment.

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to vet immediately. Do not wait even one hour.
Vaccinate with DHPPiL at 6, 8, 12, and 16 weeks — this disease is entirely preventable
Isolate infected dog completely — CDV spreads through the air
Distemper causes permanent neurological damage (seizures, twitching) even in survivors

cause: Dirofilaria immitis worms transmitted through mosquito bites. Microfilariae develop into adult worms in the heart and pulmonary arteries over 6 months causing progressive cardiovascular damage.
warning: Dogs must be tested for heartworm BEFORE starting monthly preventatives — giving prevention to a heartworm-positive dog can cause fatal anaphylactic shock.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (Pre-treatment — 4 weeks)
medicines.dose: 10 mg/kg twice daily for 4 weeks before Melarsomine treatment
medicines.note: Kills Wolbachia bacteria inside heartworms, weakening them and making treatment safer

medicines.name: Melarsomine (Immiticide) — Vet Only
medicines.dose: 2.5 mg/kg deep IM injection by vet. American Heartworm Society 3-injection protocol.
medicines.note: ABSOLUTE CAGE REST required — exercise causes dead worm pulmonary embolism which is fatal

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Cardiac + Pulmonary Support)
supplements.benefit: Reduces pulmonary arterial inflammation from dead worm debris during treatment. Supports heart function.
supplements.dose: Begin after Melarsomine treatment under vet guidance

supplements.name: Coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10) Cardiac Support
