supplements.benefit: Supports mitochondrial energy production in cardiac muscle damaged by heartworm infection
supplements.dose: 1-2 mg/kg daily with a fatty meal for best absorption

safety_warnings: Heartworm treatment requires ABSOLUTE CAGE REST — even walking to the yard can cause fatal pulmonary embolism
Prevention (monthly Heartgard/Advocate) is vastly safer and cheaper than treatment
Test dog for heartworm BEFORE starting any preventative medication
Endemic in mosquito-heavy regions — monthly prevention is mandatory year-round

cause: Stomach expands with gas and twists on itself, cutting off blood supply. Genetic predisposition in large deep-chested breeds: Great Danes, Standard Poodles, German Shepherds, Weimaraners, Dobermans.
warning: THIS IS THE #1 CANINE EMERGENCY KILLER. Every minute without treatment increases mortality. Do not drive slowly — rush to 24-hour emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: IV Fluid Shock Protocol + Emergency Surgery
medicines.dose: Large-bore IV catheter, fluid bolus for shock, trocarization to decompress gas, emergency surgery to untwist stomach
medicines.note: Without surgery mortality is nearly 100%. Time is critical.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic (Post-surgery recovery)
supplements.benefit: Restores gut microbiome after the massive disruption of GDV surgery and antibiotic treatment
supplements.dose: Begin only after discharge from vet hospital when eating has resumed

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to 24-hour emergency vet immediately. Do not wait.
Prevention: Feed 2-3 small meals daily instead of 1 large meal. No exercise 2 hours after eating.
Prophylactic gastropexy surgery during neutering recommended for all large deep-chested breeds
Rapidly expanding hard belly + retching with no vomit = GDV until proven otherwise

cause: Immune-mediated destruction of the thyroid gland (lymphocytic thyroiditis) causing slowed metabolism affecting every organ system. Common in Golden Retrievers, Dobermans, Irish Setters, Cocker Spaniels.
warning: Do NOT supplement with iodine — excess iodine triggers autoimmune thyroid attacks and worsens the condition significantly.

medicines.name: Levothyroxine (Thyrozine / Soloxine) T4
medicines.dose: 10-20 mcg/kg twice daily. Give 1 hour before food for best absorption.
medicines.note: Must be given at the same time each day. Requires T4 blood level monitoring 4-6 hours post-pill every 6 months.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Coat + Skin Recovery)
supplements.benefit: Supports skin and coat recovery from hypothyroid-related hair loss and dry skin while Levothyroxine levels stabilize
supplements.dose: Daily with food

supplements.name: Selenium + Vitamin E (Thyroid Antioxidant)
supplements.benefit: Selenium is essential for thyroid hormone conversion T4 to T3. Deficiency worsens hypothyroidism.
supplements.dose: Do NOT supplement iodine. Selenium only as directed by vet.

safety_warnings: NEVER supplement with iodine — causes autoimmune thyroid flare and worsens the condition
Levothyroxine must be given on empty stomach — food reduces absorption by up to 40%
Get a thyroid blood test for any middle-aged dog with unexplained weight gain before assuming it is just old age
Most dogs show dramatic improvement within 4-6 weeks of starting treatment

cause: Giardia duodenalis protozoa ingested through contaminated water (streams, puddles, standing water) or infected feces. Common in puppies and dogs in kennels or shelters.
warning: Giardia can be transmitted to humans especially immunocompromised individuals. Wash hands thoroughly after handling dog and feces.

medicines.name: Metronidazole (Flagyl)
medicines.dose: 25 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days with food
medicines.note: First-line for Giardia. Also treats concurrent Clostridium overgrowth. Give with food to reduce nausea.

medicines.name: Fenbendazole (Panacur)
medicines.dose: 50 mg/kg once daily for 3-5 days
medicines.note: Excellent alternative or combination with Metronidazole. Safe in puppies and pregnant dogs.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic (High-Potency Gut Recovery)
supplements.benefit: Giardia severely disrupts gut microbiome. High-dose probiotics restore lactobacillus and bifidobacteria, preventing post-Giardia SIBO.
supplements.dose: Begin during treatment and continue for minimum 4 weeks after treatment ends

safety_warnings: Zoonotic — can spread to humans. Wear gloves when handling dog feces.
Giardia cysts are resistant to most disinfectants — use quaternary ammonium compounds or bleach
Dogs must not swim in shared water sources until 2 negative fecal tests confirmed
Avoid letting dog drink from puddles, streams, or standing water

cause: Leptospira spirochete bacteria transmitted through contact with water or soil contaminated by infected wildlife urine (rats, raccoons, deer). Common near rivers, ponds, puddles, and flood water.
warning: Leptospirosis IS CONTAGIOUS TO HUMANS. Wear gloves when handling a suspected lepto dog. This is a notifiable zoonotic disease.

medicines.name: Ampicillin IV (Acute Phase — Hospital)
medicines.dose: 22 mg/kg IV every 6-8 hours for 7 days
medicines.note: Kills spirochetes rapidly. Given IV in hospital during acute severe phase.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (Carrier Phase Clearance)
medicines.dose: 5 mg/kg twice daily for 2 weeks after acute treatment
medicines.note: Critical — clears bacterial carrier state in kidneys to prevent ongoing shedding to humans

supplements.name: SAMe (Denosyl) + Milk Thistle (Liver Recovery)
supplements.benefit: Leptospirosis causes severe liver damage. SAMe replenishes glutathione and Milk Thistle regenerates liver cells — critical for hepatic recovery.
supplements.dose: Begin after acute phase stabilizes, under vet guidance

safety_warnings: Zoonotic — CONTAGIOUS TO HUMANS. Wear gloves, wash hands, report to health authority.
Vaccinate with Lepto 4 (4-serovar) vaccine annually especially near water or wildlife areas
NEVER let dogs drink from puddles, ponds, or flood water — primary source of Leptospira
EMERGENCY — severe muscle pain + fever + yellow eyes = rush to vet immediately

cause: Sudden severe immune overreaction to insect stings (bee, wasp), vaccine reactions, certain foods, or medications. Anaphylaxis involves mast cell degranulation releasing massive histamine.
warning: ANY breathing difficulty, pale gums, or collapse after allergic reaction = ANAPHYLAXIS. Rush to emergency vet immediately — minutes matter.

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl) — Mild reactions only
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg orally every 8 hours. Plain Benadryl ONLY — NOT formulations with decongestants.
medicines.note: For mild hives or face swelling with NO breathing difficulty. Any breathing issue = vet immediately.

medicines.name: Epinephrine (Adrenaline) — Anaphylaxis
medicines.dose: 0.01 mg/kg IM by vet. Must be given within minutes in true anaphylaxis.
medicines.note: Reverses bronchospasm and cardiovascular collapse. Vet only.

topical_treatments.name: Cold Compress (Sting/bite site relief)
topical_treatments.use: Wrap ice pack in towel, apply to swollen sting/bite area for 10 minutes. Reduces local histamine response and pain.

supplements.name: Quercetin (Natural Mast Cell Stabilizer — Preventative)
supplements.benefit: Inhibits mast cell histamine release. Preventative supplement for dogs with known environmental allergies. Reduces frequency of allergic reactions.
supplements.dose: 8 mg per kg twice daily with food. Take with Bromelain for enhanced absorption. NOT for acute emergency use.

safety_warnings: Breathing difficulty + pale gums + collapse = ANAPHYLAXIS EMERGENCY. Rush to vet.
NEVER give Benadryl-D or any antihistamine with decongestants — pseudoephedrine causes heart attacks in dogs
Ask vet about emergency epinephrine kit if dog has had previous anaphylactic reactions
Remove bee stinger by scraping sideways — never squeeze it as this injects more venom

cause: Demodex mites normally live harmlessly in hair follicles but overpopulate when a dog's immune system is compromised — young puppies, malnourished dogs, or dogs on immunosuppressants.
warning: Demodectic mange is NOT contagious to humans or other dogs. However, do not breed dogs with generalized Demodectic mange — it may indicate an inherited immune deficiency.

medicines.name: Fluralaner (Bravecto) Chewable Tablet
medicines.dose: Single oral dose by weight every 12 weeks. Usually 2-3 doses needed for complete resolution.
medicines.note: Most effective modern treatment for Demodectic mange. Kills mites inside hair follicles.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Pyohex Shampoo (Chlorhexidine 3% + Miconazole 1%)
topical_treatments.use: Treats secondary bacterial and fungal infections in Demodex-damaged follicles. Leave on 5-10 minutes, rinse. Use twice weekly.

supplements.name: Beaphar Multivitamine (Immune Booster — Vitamin C + Zinc)
supplements.benefit: Strengthens the immune system to suppress mite overpopulation naturally. Speeds coat regrowth.
supplements.dose: Daily with food. Immune support is critical — Demodex only explodes when immunity is weak.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Skin + Immunity)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds damaged skin barrier in affected areas and supports immune regulation
supplements.dose: Daily with food throughout treatment

safety_warnings: NOT contagious to humans or other dogs — do not isolate unnecessarily
Localized cases (small patches) in puppies often self-resolve as immune system matures
Do not breed dogs with generalized Demodectic mange — may indicate inherited immune deficiency
Secondary bacterial skin infections are common — treat with antibacterial shampoo simultaneously

cause: Caloric intake exceeding energy expenditure. Overfeeding, excessive treats, inadequate exercise, neutering (lowers metabolic rate 20-30%), hypothyroidism, or Cushing's disease.
warning: Obese dogs live 2.5 years less on average than lean dogs. Obesity is the #1 preventable health issue in dogs. Never crash-diet — rapid weight loss causes hepatic lipidosis.

medicines.name: Dirlotapide (Slentrol) — if medically indicated
medicines.dose: Given orally once daily with food. Dose titrated by vet.
medicines.note: For medically obese dogs unresponsive to diet and exercise alone. Reduces fat absorption and appetite.

supplements.name: L-Carnitine (Fat Metabolism Support)
supplements.benefit: Transports fatty acids into mitochondria for burning as energy. Preserves lean muscle mass during weight loss.
supplements.dose: 50-100 mg per day depending on body weight. Most effective combined with exercise.

supplements.name: Psyllium Husk Fiber Supplement
supplements.benefit: Creates feeling of fullness, slows digestion, regulates blood sugar. Helps dog feel satisfied on reduced calories.
supplements.dose: Mix into food. Start small and increase gradually to avoid gas. Ensure adequate water intake.

safety_warnings: Never crash-diet a dog — rapid weight loss causes hepatic lipidosis (fatty liver disease)
Target 1-2% body weight loss per week — no faster
Rule out hypothyroidism or Cushing's with blood tests if weight gain is sudden or unexplained
Measure food precisely with a scale — no free feeding or guessing portions

cause: Ingrown hair, debris embedded between toes, Staphylococcal bacterial infection, Demodex mites, allergy-driven skin inflammation, or foreign body (grass awn). Short-coated breeds most affected: Bulldogs, Labs, Boxers.
warning: NEVER squeeze or lance an interdigital cyst at home — this drives infection deeper into tissue and causes severe pain and potential abscess.

medicines.name: Cephalexin (Ceporex)
medicines.dose: 22 mg/kg twice daily for 4-6 weeks
medicines.note: Deep folliculitis requires long antibiotic courses — stopping early causes recurrence

topical_treatments.name: Betadine (Povidone-Iodine 1%) Foot Soak
topical_treatments.active: Povidone-Iodine diluted to weak tea color
topical_treatments.use: Soak affected paw 5-10 minutes twice daily. Pat dry thoroughly after. Kills bacteria and softens infected follicle.

topical_treatments.name: Epsom Salt (Magnesium Sulfate) Foot Soak
topical_treatments.use: 2 tbsp Epsom salt in 2 cups warm water. Soak paw 10 minutes twice daily. Reduces swelling and draws out infection.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Allergy + Skin Barrier)
supplements.benefit: Addresses allergic skin inflammation — the #1 root cause of recurrent interdigital cysts in predisposed breeds
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: NEVER squeeze or lance the cyst at home — drives infection deeper
Use E-collar to prevent licking — moisture from licking prevents healing
Check for embedded grass awns (foxtails) — these migrate deep and require surgery
Dry paws thoroughly after every walk — moisture promotes bacterial growth

cause: Insufficient fiber in diet leading to soft stools that don't naturally express the glands. Obesity, narrow anal sac ducts, food allergies, and poor muscle tone also contribute.
warning: Do NOT attempt home anal gland expression without proper training — incorrect technique ruptures the gland or pushes infection deeper causing abscess.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (if anal sac abscess develops)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days
medicines.note: For infected or abscessed anal sacs only. Requires manual drainage or flushing by vet first.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Saline Compress
topical_treatments.use: Apply warm damp cloth to the anal area for 5-10 minutes twice daily. Reduces pain and inflammation, may encourage natural drainage.

supplements.name: Psyllium Husk Fiber Supplement
supplements.benefit: Increases stool bulk and firmness. Firmer stools naturally express anal glands with each bowel movement, preventing impaction.
supplements.dose: 1 tsp per 10 kg daily mixed into wet food. Ensure adequate water intake. Increase gradually.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Anti-inflammatory for ducts)
supplements.benefit: Reduces inflammation in anal sac ducts, making them less prone to swelling and blockage
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Never express anal glands at home without training — can rupture the gland
Recurrent impaction needs vet workup for allergies or diet issues — not just repeated expressions
An anal sac abscess will rupture if untreated — see vet before this happens

cause: Secondary to kennel cough or dog flu, aspiration of food/vomit (aspiration pneumonia), bacterial infection (Bordetella, Streptococcus), or fungal infection. Compromises oxygen exchange in the lungs.
warning: Blue or purple gums = oxygen deprivation EMERGENCY. Go to vet immediately — do not wait.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate + Enrofloxacin (Combination)
medicines.dose: Per vet protocol based on culture. Duration minimum 3-6 weeks.
medicines.note: Combination covers gram-positive, gram-negative, and atypical bacteria causing pneumonia

topical_treatments.name: Saline Nebulization + Coupage
topical_treatments.use: Inhaled saline loosens thick mucus. Coupage (rhythmic chest clapping) mobilizes secretions for coughing out. Done by vet nurses in hospital. Can continue at home with human nebulizer 3-4x daily.

supplements.name: N-Acetylcysteine (NAC) — Mucolytic
supplements.benefit: Directly breaks bonds in lung mucus, dramatically reducing viscosity. Makes coughing more effective and speeds clearance of infected material.
supplements.dose: Oral during home recovery phase under vet guidance

supplements.name: Vitamin C High-Dose (Lung Tissue Protection)
supplements.benefit: Reduces oxidative damage to lung alveolar cells, supports white blood cell function, speeds recovery from pneumonia
supplements.dose: 125 mg per 5 kg twice daily during active pneumonia. Reduce to maintenance after recovery.

safety_warnings: Blue or purple gums = EMERGENCY — go to vet immediately
Strict rest for minimum 3-4 weeks even after symptoms resolve — lungs need time to heal
Aspiration pneumonia risk is highest in vomiting dogs, those under sedation, or with megaesophagus

cause: Idiopathic vestibular syndrome (most common — unknown cause, resolves on its own), inner ear infection, hypothyroidism, or rarely brain tumor. Very alarming appearance but often not serious.
warning: Idiopathic vestibular disease looks like a stroke but usually RESOLVES on its own. Most dogs improve significantly within 72 hours. See vet to rule out ear infection and brain tumor.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) Anti-nausea
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg once daily for 3-5 days
medicines.note: Controls profound nausea and vomiting from vestibular-induced vertigo. Significantly improves quality of life.

medicines.name: Meclizine (OTC Motion Sickness Tablet)
medicines.dose: 25 mg per medium/large dog once daily
medicines.note: Reduces spinning sensation from vestibular dysfunction. Mild sedative effect also helps.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 + B-Vitamin Complex
supplements.benefit: DHA in Omega-3 supports myelin repair in the vestibular nerve. B vitamins (B6, B12) support nerve function recovery.
supplements.dose: Begin from day 1 of recovery. Continue for 4-8 weeks.

safety_warnings: Prevent falls — create a padded area and assist walking. Keep away from stairs and elevated surfaces.
See vet to rule out ear infection, hypothyroidism, and brain tumor before assuming idiopathic
If no improvement at 72 hours — see vet immediately as brain tumor becomes more likely
Do not leave dog alone near water sources during vestibular episode

cause: Canine influenza virus H3N2 (predominant) or H3N8. Spread through respiratory droplets and contaminated surfaces. Can sweep through kennels and dog parks rapidly with presymptomatic shedding.
warning: Isolate the infected dog completely from other dogs — dog flu spreads before symptoms appear. Seek vet if labored breathing develops — pneumonia can develop rapidly.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (Secondary bacterial pneumonia prevention)
medicines.dose: 5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-14 days with food
medicines.note: For secondary bacterial bronchopneumonia which is the main cause of mortality in dog flu

topical_treatments.name: Cool Mist Humidifier
topical_treatments.use: Run in dog's resting area throughout recovery. Moisturizes inflamed airways, loosens secretions, reduces coughing. Keep water tank clean to prevent mold.

supplements.name: Zesty Paws Immune Bites (Elderberry + Vitamin C + Zinc)
supplements.benefit: Elderberry flavonoids block viral cell entry. Vitamin C + Zinc boost natural killer cell activity. Reduces viral duration and severity.
supplements.dose: Safe during infection. Do not exceed recommended dose of Zinc — excess is toxic to dogs.

safety_warnings: Isolate completely — dog flu spreads before symptoms appear (presymptomatic shedding)
Vaccinate social dogs annually especially those visiting kennels, dog parks, or groomers
Seek vet if fever exceeds 40°C or dog develops labored breathing — pneumonia risk is high
Disinfect all shared surfaces, bowls, and bedding with quaternary ammonium disinfectant

aliases: dog hair loss trunk muscle wasting
cause: Pituitary gland tumor (85% of cases) stimulating excess ACTH causing adrenal glands to overproduce cortisol. Or adrenal tumor directly. Iatrogenic Cushing's from long-term steroid use.
warning: NEVER abruptly stop Trilostane — can trigger an Addisonian crisis (life-threatening adrenal failure). Dogs on long-term steroids must also never stop suddenly.

medicines.name: Trilostane (Vetoryl)
medicines.dose: 2-10 mg/kg once daily with food. Dose titrated by ACTH stimulation test.
medicines.note: Gold-standard treatment. Never stop suddenly — taper under vet supervision only.

supplements.name: Melatonin (Adrenal Function Modulator)
supplements.benefit: At specific doses, melatonin inhibits adrenal steroid production and can reduce cortisol. Useful adjunct in mild cases.
supplements.dose: 3 mg for dogs under 30 kg, 6 mg for over 30 kg, twice daily. Only under vet supervision. Avoid xylitol formulations.

supplements.name: Flaxseed Hull Lignans
supplements.benefit: Plant lignans inhibit an enzyme involved in adrenal steroid production. Shown to reduce cortisol. Often combined with Melatonin.
supplements.dose: Per body weight under vet guidance. Often used as a natural adjunct to Trilostane.

safety_warnings: NEVER abruptly stop Trilostane — causes Addisonian crisis (life-threatening adrenal failure)
Watch for Addison's disease signs on Trilostane: sudden weakness, vomiting, collapse
Dogs on long-term steroids (more than 6 weeks) must never stop steroids suddenly
Recurring skin infections + pot belly + extreme thirst = strong Cushing's suspicion — blood test needed

cause: Dogs chew and swallow non-food objects — especially puppies. High-risk items: corn cobs, socks, toys, bones, rubber balls, bottle caps, fruit pits, hair ties.
warning: DO NOT give hydrogen peroxide at home to induce vomiting — causes severe hemorrhagic gastroenteritis in dogs. Only a vet can safely induce vomiting.

medicines.name: Apomorphine (Induced Vomiting) — Vet Only
medicines.dose: 0.04 mg/kg IV or conjunctival. Only if ingestion within 2 hours and object is not sharp.
medicines.note: NEVER induce vomiting at home for sharp objects, caustic chemicals, or ingestion more than 2 hours ago.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora + Pancreatin Enzymes (Post-surgery)
supplements.benefit: Restores gut microbiome and digestive function after intestinal surgery and antibiotic treatment
supplements.dose: Begin when eating resumes post-surgery. Use for minimum 4-6 weeks.

safety_warnings: DO NOT give hydrogen peroxide at home to induce vomiting — causes hemorrhagic gastroenteritis
DO NOT wait and see if symptoms are present — intestinal perforation is fatal within 24 hours
Keep socks, children's toys, corn cobs, and fruit pits out of dog reach at all times
No bowel movement in 24 hours + vomiting + lethargy = possible obstruction. Emergency vet.

cause: Scratch to the cornea from a cat claw, thorn, grass awn, or from rubbing. Brachycephalic breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, French Bulldogs) have protruding eyes highly prone to corneal ulcers.
warning: A corneal ulcer can perforate the eye within 24-48 hours if untreated. This causes permanent vision loss or loss of the eye. Do not delay vet visit.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Antibiotic Eye Drops + Atropine
medicines.dose: Tobramycin 1-2 drops every 4-6 hours. Atropine 1 drop twice daily for pain relief.
medicines.note: Atropine dilates pupil reducing painful ciliary muscle spasm. Never use steroid drops on a corneal ulcer — causes perforation.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye Flush 0.9%
topical_treatments.use: Gently flush eye to remove debris before applying drops. Use before every drop application.

supplements.name: Vitamin C + Omega-3 (Corneal Healing Support)
supplements.benefit: Vitamin C is essential for collagen synthesis in corneal repair. Omega-3 reduces ocular inflammation during healing.
supplements.dose: 125 mg Vitamin C per 5 kg daily during healing period

safety_warnings: NEVER use steroid eye drops on a corneal ulcer — causes perforation and permanent blindness
NEVER use human Visine or any OTC eye drops not specifically for corneal ulcers
Use E-collar immediately to prevent rubbing — rubbing converts a simple ulcer into a perforated one
Brachycephalic breeds need immediate vet attention — their ulcers progress faster than other breeds

cause: Abnormal proliferation of mast cells. Strong genetic predisposition in Boxers, Bulldogs, Golden Retrievers, Labrador Retrievers, Pugs, and Boston Terriers. Can develop at any age.
warning: ALL new skin lumps should be tested with fine-needle aspirate (FNA). NEVER squeeze or massage a skin lump — if it is a mast cell tumor, this causes massive histamine release and anaphylaxis.

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl) — Pre-surgical antihistamine
medicines.dispensing: OTC at surgical dose per vet
medicines.dose: 2 mg/kg before and after any manipulation of the tumor
medicines.note: Prevents histamine crisis (anaphylaxis) when mast cells are disrupted during surgery or examination

medicines.name: Toceranib Phosphate (Palladia) — Targeted Therapy
medicines.dose: Per oncology protocol. Every other day dosing.
medicines.note: For Grade 2-3 or recurrent MCTs with c-KIT mutation. 40-60% response rate.

supplements.name: Quercetin (Natural Mast Cell Stabilizer)
supplements.benefit: Inhibits mast cell degranulation, reducing histamine release from the tumor. Synergistic with antihistamine treatment.
supplements.dose: 8 mg per kg twice daily with food. Use alongside conventional treatment, not instead of it.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 EPA+DHA (Anti-tumor Inflammation)
supplements.benefit: EPA inhibits pro-inflammatory prostaglandins that promote tumor growth. Improves outcomes alongside chemotherapy.
supplements.dose: High dose by weight daily

safety_warnings: ALL new skin lumps need fine-needle aspirate test — mast cell tumors look identical to benign lipomas
NEVER squeeze or massage any skin lump — histamine release causes anaphylaxis
Darier's sign (skin wheal and redness when lump is rubbed) is highly suspicious for MCT — tell vet immediately
Surgical excision with wide margins is the primary treatment — early surgery = best prognosis

cause: Prolonged cold or wet exposure without shelter, falling into cold water, anesthesia complications, or illness reducing heat production. Small dogs, puppies, senior dogs, and thin-coated breeds are at extreme risk.
warning: When shivering stops in a still-cold dog, this is a DANGER SIGN — not improvement. The body has run out of energy to shiver. Rush to vet immediately.

medicines.name: IV Warm Fluids (Internal Rewarming — Vet Only)
medicines.dose: Warmed IV saline at 40°C delivered by vet. Fastest and safest internal rewarming method.
medicines.note: External rewarming is slower — always get vet care for temperatures below 33°C.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Water Bottles + Thermal Blanket (Home First Aid)
topical_treatments.active: Warm water bottles wrapped in towels
topical_treatments.use: Place warm (NOT hot) water bottles wrapped in towels in armpits and groin. Fan with warm air. Replace bottles every 15 minutes. Stop active rewarming at 37.5°C to prevent rebound hyperthermia.

supplements.name: NutriCal High-Calorie Recovery Paste
supplements.benefit: Provides rapid high-calorie glucose and fat to fuel the body's heat production during hypothermia recovery. Prevents hypoglycemia that accompanies hypothermia.
supplements.dose: Small amount on finger — allow dog to lick freely if conscious. Do NOT force-feed to unresponsive dog.

safety_warnings: NEVER use electric heating pads directly on skin — hypothermic dogs cannot sense burns
Shivering stopping in a still-cold dog = DANGER SIGN. Rush to vet.
NEVER warm too quickly — rapid rewarming causes dangerous heart arrhythmias
Small dogs, puppies, and senior dogs get hypothermic at temperatures other dogs tolerate — use dog jackets

cause: Common dog toxins: chocolate (theobromine), grapes/raisins (unknown toxin — kidney failure), xylitol (sugar-free gum/peanut butter — liver failure), rat poison (anticoagulant), ibuprofen, paracetamol, onions, macadamia nuts, alcohol.
warning: Do NOT induce vomiting at home without vet instruction — for caustic substances, sharp objects, or certain toxins, vomiting causes more damage. Call ASPCA Poison Control or your vet immediately.

medicines.name: Apomorphine (Induced Vomiting) — Vet Only
medicines.dose: Only if ingestion within 1-2 hours and toxin is not caustic or sharp
medicines.note: Timing is critical — vomiting after 2 hours is ineffective as toxin is already absorbed

medicines.name: Activated Charcoal (Toxin Binding)
medicines.dose: 1-4 g/kg orally by vet after vomiting is controlled
medicines.note: Binds many toxins in the gut before absorption. Not effective for all toxins (e.g., alcohol, xylitol).

supplements.name: SAMe (Denosyl) + Milk Thistle (Liver Protection)
supplements.benefit: For toxins causing liver damage (xylitol, paracetamol, mushrooms). SAMe replenishes glutathione and Milk Thistle regenerates liver cells during recovery.
supplements.dose: Begin under vet guidance after acute phase. Not a substitute for emergency treatment.

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — call vet or ASPCA Poison Control immediately. Do not wait for symptoms.
NEVER give milk, salt water, or hydrogen peroxide at home without vet instruction
Keep ALL human medications, xylitol products, grapes, chocolate, and onions out of dog reach
Bring the packaging of what the dog ate to the vet — helps identify exact toxin and dose

cause: Weakness of the connective tissue anchoring the tear gland of the third eyelid, allowing it to prolapse and appear as a red mass in the inner corner of the eye. Common in Bulldogs, Beagles, Cocker Spaniels, and Bloodhounds.
warning: Do NOT attempt to push the gland back yourself — this risks permanent damage to the gland. This gland produces 30-40% of the eye's tear film — removing it causes permanent dry eye.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops (Prevent secondary infection)
medicines.dose: 1-2 drops in affected eye 3 times daily until surgery
medicines.note: Prevents bacterial infection of the exposed prolapsed gland tissue before surgical correction

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Lubricating Eye Drops (Artificial Tears)
topical_treatments.use: Apply 1-2 drops to keep prolapsed gland moist before surgical correction. Prevents drying and damage to the gland tissue.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Tear Film Support)
supplements.benefit: Supports healthy tear film production and reduces ocular surface inflammation. Important for long-term eye health after cherry eye surgery.
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: NEVER remove the cherry eye gland surgically — it produces 30-40% of tear film. Removal causes permanent dry eye.
Surgical repositioning (pocket technique) is the correct procedure — not removal
Without treatment, the exposed gland becomes chronically inflamed and damaged
Both eyes are often affected — even if only one shows cherry eye initially

cause: Accumulation of beta-amyloid plaques in the brain (similar to Alzheimer's in humans) causing progressive cognitive decline. Affects dogs over 10 years old — 50% of dogs over 15 have significant CCD.
warning: CCD is progressive and incurable but highly manageable. Environmental enrichment, diet, and supplements significantly slow progression and maintain quality of life.

medicines.name: Selegiline (Anipryl / Selgian)
medicines.dose: 0.5-1 mg/kg once daily in the morning
medicines.note: Only FDA-approved drug for CCD. Increases dopamine in the brain. Takes 4-6 weeks to see effect.

supplements.name: MCT Oil (Medium Chain Triglycerides — Brain Fuel)
supplements.benefit: Provides alternative ketone fuel source for neurons when glucose metabolism fails in aging brain. Studies show significant cognitive improvement.
supplements.dose: Start 0.5 ml/kg daily, increase over 2-4 weeks to 1-2 ml/kg. Too much causes diarrhea.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 DHA (Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: DHA is the primary structural fat in brain cell membranes. High-dose DHA slows amyloid plaque formation and supports neuronal communication.
supplements.dose: High dose by weight daily. Start early — before significant symptoms appear for best effect.

supplements.name: SAMe (S-Adenosyl Methionine) — Denosyl
supplements.benefit: SAMe is essential for neurotransmitter synthesis and brain cell membrane health. Studies show improved cognitive scores in dogs with CCD.
supplements.dose: Give on empty stomach 1-2 hours before food. Do not crush enteric coating.

safety_warnings: CCD is progressive — start supplements and medication EARLY for best effect
Maintain consistent daily routine — changes in schedule significantly worsen confusion
Night wandering and vocalization is common — create a safe confined sleeping area
Rule out pain (arthritis), hearing loss, and vision loss before attributing all symptoms to CCD

cause: Most commonly caused by plaque and tartar buildup on teeth (periodontal disease). Can also be caused by kidney disease (ammonia smell), liver disease (sweet/musty smell), diabetes (fruity smell), or eating feces/garbage.
warning: Very sweet or fruity smelling breath = possible diabetes. Ammonia or urine smell = possible kidney disease. These need immediate vet blood tests.

medicines.name: Clindamycin (only if gum infection confirmed)
medicines.dose: 5.5 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: Only prescribed if there is active gum infection. Bad breath from tartar needs professional dental cleaning first.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac C.E.T. Enzymatic Dog Toothpaste
topical_treatments.use: Brush teeth daily or minimum 3 times per week. NEVER use human toothpaste — xylitol and fluoride are toxic to dogs.

topical_treatments.name: Tropiclean Fresh Breath Water Additive
topical_treatments.use: Add 1 capful per 250ml of drinking water daily. Tasteless and odorless — dogs accept it easily. Reduces plaque and bacteria.

supplements.name: Probiotic (Purina Fortiflora)
supplements.benefit: Balances oral and gut bacteria that contribute to bad breath. Reduces sulfur-producing bacteria in the mouth.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily sprinkled on food

safety_warnings: NEVER use human toothpaste — xylitol is toxic and causes liver failure in dogs
Sweet fruity breath = possible diabetes. Ammonia breath = possible kidney disease. See vet for blood tests.
Professional dental cleaning under anesthesia is needed to remove tartar under the gum line
Start dental care from puppyhood — prevention is far easier than treating advanced disease

cause: Seasonal shedding is normal. Abnormal excessive shedding can be caused by poor nutrition, stress, allergies, parasites (fleas, mites), hormonal imbalance (hypothyroidism, Cushing's), or pregnancy.
warning: If bald patches appear with no regrowth, or shedding is sudden and severe — get a vet blood test to rule out hypothyroidism or Cushing's disease.

medicines.name: No specific medicine for normal shedding
medicines.dose: Focus on diet, supplements, and grooming. Medicine only needed if underlying hormonal disease is found.
medicines.note: If hypothyroidism or Cushing's is confirmed — specific medication prescribed by vet

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina Coat Conditioner Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Bathe once a week. Massage well into coat, leave 3 minutes, rinse thoroughly. Strengthens hair follicles and reduces breakage.

topical_treatments.name: Furminator Deshedding Brush
topical_treatments.use: Brush 2-3 times weekly to remove loose undercoat before it falls on furniture. Reduces shedding by up to 90%.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Coat Health)
supplements.benefit: Omega-3 fatty acids strengthen hair follicles, reduce inflammation causing hair fall, and add shine to the coat. Most effective supplement for shedding.
supplements.dose: Daily with food. Results visible in coat quality within 4-6 weeks.

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7) Supplement
supplements.benefit: Biotin is essential for keratin production — the protein that makes up hair. Reduces breakage and promotes thicker coat regrowth.
supplements.dose: Once daily with food for minimum 6-8 weeks to see results

safety_warnings: Bald patches with no regrowth = see vet for thyroid and hormone blood tests
Sudden heavy shedding after illness, surgery, or delivery is normal (telogen effluvium) and resolves in 2-3 months
Never shave a double-coated breed (Huskies, Goldens) — destroys the natural insulation and coat may not regrow correctly
Poor diet is the #1 cause of abnormal shedding — switch to high-quality protein-rich food

cause: Low humidity in winter, over-bathing with harsh shampoos, poor diet lacking fatty acids, allergies, hypothyroidism, or parasites (Cheyletiella — walking dandruff mite).
warning: If dandruff is moving (literally looks like it is walking) — this is Cheyletiella mites, a contagious parasite. Needs antiparasitic treatment, not moisturiser.

medicines.name: No specific medicine for simple dry skin
medicines.dose: Focus on diet, supplements, and correct shampoo. Medicated treatment only if underlying cause found.
medicines.note: If Cheyletiella mites confirmed — treat with Fipronil spot-on or ivermectin as directed by vet

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Allermyl Moisturising Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Use once a week maximum. Leave on 5 minutes before rinsing. Over-bathing worsens dry skin.

topical_treatments.name: Coconut Oil (Topical Moisturiser)
topical_treatments.use: Warm a small amount between palms and massage gently into dry skin patches after bath. Safe if dog licks it.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Skin Hydration)
supplements.benefit: EPA and DHA rebuild the skin's natural oil barrier from inside, reducing water loss through the skin and eliminating flakiness.
supplements.dose: Daily with food. Most effective supplement for dry skin — results in 3-4 weeks.

safety_warnings: Do NOT bathe more than once a week — over-bathing strips natural oils causing more dryness
Moving dandruff (walking dandruff) = Cheyletiella mites, not dry skin — needs antiparasitic treatment
NEVER use human moisturisers or lotions — many contain chemicals toxic to dogs when licked
Add omega-3 to diet before trying expensive shampoos — diet is the root cause in 70% of cases

cause: Bile accumulates in an empty stomach and irritates the lining, causing vomiting of yellow or greenish foam — usually in the early morning before breakfast. Common in dogs fed once daily.
warning: If yellow vomiting happens more than 2-3 times per week, or if the dog also has diarrhea, lethargy, or weight loss — see vet to rule out liver disease, pancreatitis, or inflammatory bowel disease.

medicines.name: Famotidine (Pepcid AC) — OTC
medicines.dose: 0.5-1 mg/kg once or twice daily before meals
medicines.note: Reduces stomach acid production. Plain Famotidine only — not formulations with other ingredients.

medicines.name: Omeprazole (Prilosec) — OTC
medicines.dose: 0.5-1 mg/kg once daily before breakfast
medicines.note: More powerful acid suppression than Famotidine. Give on empty stomach 30 minutes before first meal.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Supports healthy gut bacteria and reduces gastric irritation from bile. Helps regulate stomach acid production.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily with food

safety_warnings: Simplest fix: give a small snack before bed so the stomach is not completely empty overnight
Switch from once-daily to twice-daily feeding — this alone resolves most cases
Blood or brown coffee-ground material in vomit = emergency vet immediately
If vomiting is frequent — rule out pancreatitis and IBD with vet blood tests before assuming it is just bile

cause: Most commonly early osteoarthritis — joints stiffen during rest and loosen up with movement. Also caused by patellar luxation, hip dysplasia, old injury, or Lyme disease in tick-endemic areas.
warning: If stiffness is sudden, severe, or accompanied by fever and swollen joints — see vet to rule out immune-mediated polyarthritis or Lyme disease.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food
medicines.note: NEVER give ibuprofen, aspirin, or paracetamol — all are toxic and potentially fatal to dogs

topical_treatments.name: Warm Compress on Stiff Joints
topical_treatments.use: Apply warm damp cloth to stiff joints for 10-15 minutes before walks. Improves blood flow and reduces morning stiffness significantly.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds joint cartilage and lubricates joints. Reduces stiffness over time. Safe for lifelong daily use.
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks. Allow 6-8 weeks before judging effectiveness.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Joint Inflammation)
supplements.benefit: Reduces inflammatory mediators in joint fluid that cause stiffness and pain. Works synergistically with Glucosamine.
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: NEVER give human painkillers — ibuprofen and paracetamol cause fatal kidney failure in dogs
Maintain lean body weight — excess weight is the single biggest aggravating factor for joint stiffness
Short gentle walks are better than long walks for arthritic dogs — little and often
Get an orthopedic memory foam bed — hard floors dramatically worsen stiffness

cause: Common causes: diabetes mellitus, kidney disease, Cushing's disease, pyometra (intact females), liver disease, hypercalcemia, or certain medications (steroids, diuretics). Always needs blood and urine tests to find the cause.
warning: Excessive thirst + urination is NEVER normal. It always indicates an underlying medical condition that must be diagnosed. Do not reduce water intake — the dog needs it.

medicines.name: Depends entirely on underlying diagnosis
medicines.dose: Blood tests + urine tests + ultrasound needed before any medication is prescribed
medicines.note: Do NOT restrict water. The dog drinks more because something is wrong — water is keeping it alive.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Kidney + Organ Support)
supplements.benefit: Supports kidney and liver function while waiting for diagnosis. Safe to give regardless of which organ is affected.
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: NEVER restrict water access — the dog needs it and restricting causes serious harm
Always see a vet — excessive thirst is a symptom, not a disease, and always has a treatable underlying cause
Intact female dogs with excessive thirst may have pyometra (infected uterus) — life-threatening emergency
Bring a urine sample to the vet appointment — collect the first morning urine in a clean container

cause: Clear discharge: allergies, excitement, or mild irritation. Yellow or green discharge: bacterial or fungal infection. Blood-tinged discharge: foreign body in nose, nasal tumor, or fungal infection (Aspergillosis). One nostril only: foreign body or tumor.
warning: Blood from only ONE nostril in a middle-aged to older dog = possible nasal tumor or Aspergillus fungal infection. See vet urgently for scoping and imaging.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (bacterial nasal infection)
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily for 10-14 days with food
medicines.note: For confirmed bacterial rhinitis. Culture needed for chronic or non-responding cases.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Nasal Flush
topical_treatments.use: Gently flush one nostril at a time using a syringe or dropper. Removes debris and mucus. Use 2-3 times daily for congestion.

supplements.name: Vitamin C + Zinc Immune Support
supplements.benefit: Supports immune response against respiratory infections. Reduces severity and duration of nasal infections.
supplements.dose: 125 mg Vitamin C per 5 kg body weight daily during illness

safety_warnings: Blood from one nostril in an older dog = possible tumor or fungal infection. See vet urgently.
Never use human nasal decongestants (Otrivin, Nasivion) — contain xylometazoline which is toxic to dogs
Brachycephalic breeds (Pugs, Bulldogs) have structurally narrow nostrils — chronic discharge is common but still needs monitoring
Persistent green/yellow discharge for more than 7 days always needs antibiotic treatment

cause: Dehydration (most common), low fiber diet, ingested hair or foreign material, lack of exercise, painful anal glands, enlarged prostate (intact males), or side effect of certain medications.
warning: If dog has not had a bowel movement in more than 48 hours AND is straining hard with no output — see vet. Obstipation (complete blockage) requires enema or manual removal by vet.

medicines.name: Lactulose (Stool Softener)
medicines.dose: 0.5 ml/kg 2-3 times daily mixed into food. Reduce dose when stools normalize.
medicines.note: Safe and gentle. Works by drawing water into the colon to soften stool. Takes 12-24 hours to work.

supplements.name: Psyllium Husk Fiber (Natural Stool Softener)
supplements.benefit: Absorbs water and adds bulk to stool, making it easier to pass. Safe for long-term daily use as dietary fiber.
supplements.dose: 1 tsp per 10 kg daily mixed into wet food or water. Always increase water intake alongside.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores healthy gut bacteria balance which regulates bowel movement frequency and stool consistency.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily with food

safety_warnings: NEVER give human laxatives (Dulcolax, Castor oil, Senna) — these cause dangerous cramping and electrolyte loss in dogs
Always increase water intake when treating constipation — dehydration is the most common cause
No bowel movement in 48+ hours with straining = see vet for possible enema
Intact male dogs over 5 years: constipation may indicate enlarged prostate — get checked

cause: Lymph nodes swell in response to: local infection near the node (most common), systemic infection (tick disease, Leptospirosis), immune-mediated disease, or most seriously — lymphoma (cancer of the lymphatic system).
warning: Swollen lymph nodes that are firm, painless, and involve multiple nodes simultaneously = POSSIBLE LYMPHOMA. This is one of the most common cancers in dogs. See vet immediately for fine-needle aspirate.

medicines.name: Diagnosis required before any treatment
medicines.dose: FNA (fine-needle aspirate) of the enlarged node is required to distinguish infection from cancer before prescribing anything
medicines.note: Treating presumed infection with antibiotics when it is actually lymphoma wastes critical treatment time

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Immune + Anti-inflammatory)
supplements.benefit: Reduces inflammatory cytokines. Supports immune regulation whether cause is infection or immune-mediated disease.
supplements.dose: Daily with food while awaiting diagnosis

safety_warnings: Multiple firm painless swollen lymph nodes = possible lymphoma. See vet immediately — do not delay.
Never massage swollen lymph nodes — if it is cancer, this can disrupt the tumor
Lymphoma in dogs is treatable with chemotherapy — early diagnosis significantly improves outcomes
Single warm painful node near a wound = likely local infection. Multiple painless nodes = more serious.

cause: Sudden irritation of the soft palate and nasopharynx causes rapid repeated inhalations producing a loud honking or snorting sound. Triggered by excitement, eating too fast, perfume, dust, pollen, pulling on collar, or post-nasal drip. Common in small breeds and brachycephalic dogs.
warning: Reverse sneezing looks alarming but is almost always completely harmless. However — if episodes are very frequent, last more than 2 minutes, or are getting worse over time — see vet to rule out nasal polyp or foreign body.

medicines.name: No medication needed for typical reverse sneezing
medicines.dose: Most episodes stop within 30-60 seconds on their own
medicines.note: Cetirizine (Zyrtec) 0.5 mg/kg once daily may help if allergies are the trigger. Plain Cetirizine only — not formulations with decongestants.

topical_treatments.name: Home Management During Episode
topical_treatments.use: During episode: gently cover both nostrils briefly with your finger for 1-2 seconds to trigger swallowing, which resets the soft palate. Or gently massage the throat. Stay calm — excitement prolongs episodes.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Allergy + Inflammation)
supplements.benefit: If reverse sneezing is allergy-triggered, Omega-3 reduces the nasal and throat inflammation that causes the episodes. Reduces frequency over time.
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: Reverse sneezing is almost always harmless — do not panic. Stay calm so the dog stays calm.
Use a harness instead of a neck collar — collar pressure on the throat is a common trigger
Episodes getting more frequent or lasting longer = see vet to rule out nasal polyp or foreign body
NEVER use human decongestant sprays in dog's nose — they contain chemicals toxic to dogs

cause: Common causes: stress or anxiety (new home, new pet, owner travel), nausea from medication, dental pain, upset stomach, hot weather reducing appetite, or underlying illness. Puppies and small dogs can develop dangerous low blood sugar (hypoglycemia) within hours of not eating.
warning: Puppies and small breed dogs that refuse food for more than 12 hours can develop hypoglycemia (low blood sugar) — a life-threatening emergency. Watch for trembling, weakness, or collapse.

medicines.name: Mirtazapine (Appetite Stimulant)
medicines.dose: 1.88 mg per cat / 1-2 mg/kg for dogs every 3 days
medicines.note: Used when not eating is due to nausea or underlying illness. Find and treat the root cause first.

supplements.name: NutriCal High-Calorie Paste (Emergency Nutrition)
supplements.benefit: High-calorie glucose and fat supplement to prevent dangerous hypoglycemia in puppies and small dogs refusing food
supplements.dose: Small amount on finger, allow dog to lick. Give every 4-6 hours if dog is not eating.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic (Appetite + Gut)
supplements.benefit: Sprinkled on food, the palatability of Fortiflora encourages picky or nauseous dogs to eat. Also restores gut balance reducing nausea.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet sprinkled on food at each meal

safety_warnings: Puppy not eating for more than 12 hours = hypoglycemia risk. Give NutriCal and see vet.
Never force-feed a vomiting dog — aspiration pneumonia risk
Try warming the food slightly — smell is a major appetite trigger for dogs
Not eating for more than 48 hours in an adult dog always needs vet evaluation

cause: Occasional grass eating is completely normal dog behavior — not a sign of illness. Theories include instinctive gut cleaning, fiber supplementation, boredom, or nausea relief. Eating non-food items (dirt, rocks, plastic) more seriously may indicate mineral deficiency or obsessive compulsive behavior.
warning: If dog eats large quantities of grass then vomits repeatedly — check for obstruction or severe gastroenteritis. Eating rocks or sharp objects can cause intestinal perforation.

medicines.name: No medicine needed for normal grass eating
medicines.dose: Occasional grass eating is normal and harmless. Only investigate if obsessive or involves harmful items.
medicines.note: If eating rocks or hard objects — see vet immediately for X-ray to check for obstruction

supplements.name: Psyllium Husk Fiber
supplements.benefit: Adding fiber to diet reduces the urge to eat grass as dogs instinctively seek fiber when their diet lacks it
supplements.dose: 1 tsp per 10 kg mixed into food daily

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Balances gut bacteria — dogs with digestive imbalance eat grass more frequently. Probiotics reduce this urge.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily with food

safety_warnings: Ensure grass the dog eats is NOT treated with pesticides, herbicides, or fertilizers — these are toxic
Eating rocks, plastic, or sharp objects is dangerous — can cause intestinal perforation. See vet.
Obsessive pica (compulsive non-food eating) may indicate anxiety or OCD — consult a vet behaviorist
Always deworm regularly — parasite burden increases grass-eating behavior

cause: Hypersensitivity to sudden loud sounds (fireworks, thunderstorms, crackers, vehicles backfiring). Dogs hear frequencies 4x better than humans — loud sounds are genuinely painful to them. Worsens with age and each exposure if not managed.
warning: Dogs in extreme panic can injure themselves trying to escape — jumping through windows, breaking through doors, or running into traffic. Secure the environment before fireworks events.

medicines.name: Trazodone (Situational anxiety — as needed)
medicines.dose: 2-5 mg/kg every 6-8 hours, given 1-2 hours before the anticipated event
medicines.note: Works within 1-2 hours. Ideal for Diwali, New Year, thunderstorm seasons. Ask vet in advance.

medicines.name: Acepromazine — DO NOT USE
medicines.dose: DO NOT USE for noise phobia
medicines.note: Acepromazine sedates the body but NOT the mind — the dog is still terrified but cannot move. This worsens noise phobia long-term.

topical_treatments.name: Adaptil DAP Diffuser + Spray
topical_treatments.use: Spray on dog's bedding 15 minutes before event. Plug diffuser into main room. Clinically proven to reduce noise phobia severity by 30-50%.

supplements.name: Zesty Paws Calming Bites (L-Theanine + Melatonin)
supplements.benefit: L-Theanine raises GABA and serotonin producing calm without sedation. Give 30-60 minutes before expected fireworks for best effect.
supplements.dose: As directed on packaging by weight. Can be used for every noise event.

safety_warnings: NEVER use Acepromazine for noise phobia — sedates body but not mind, worsens fear long-term
Secure all doors, windows, and gates before fireworks — panicking dogs escape and run into traffic
NEVER punish or scold a fearful dog — punishment dramatically worsens phobia
Create a safe den (covered crate with familiar bedding) where dog can hide — respect their need to hide

cause: Nuclear Sclerosis: normal aging change in dogs over 7 years — lens fibers compress making the eye appear bluish-grey. Does NOT significantly impair vision. Cataracts: true opacity of the lens causing vision loss. Diabetes causes rapid cataract formation in both eyes.
warning: If the dog is bumping into furniture or walls, or is reluctant to navigate stairs in dim light — this is vision loss from cataracts, not just nuclear sclerosis. See vet for proper diagnosis.

medicines.name: Topical NSAID Eye Drops (pre/post cataract surgery)
medicines.dose: 1 drop 3-4 times daily as directed by veterinary ophthalmologist
medicines.note: Used before and after cataract surgery to prevent uveitis (inflammation). Not for general use.

topical_treatments.name: Ocluvet Eye Drops (N-Acetyl-Carnosine)
topical_treatments.use: 1-2 drops in each eye twice daily. May slow early cataract progression. Use before surgery becomes necessary.

supplements.name: Ocu-Glo (Lutein + Zeaxanthin + Omega-3)
supplements.benefit: Lutein and Zeaxanthin concentrate in the lens and retina, neutralizing free radicals that cause cataract formation. Slows early progression.
supplements.dose: Daily with food. Best started early as a preventative in dogs over 6 years.

safety_warnings: Nuclear sclerosis (normal aging blue haze) does NOT need treatment — it barely affects vision
Cataracts from diabetes progress extremely rapidly — blood sugar control is the most important intervention
Untreated mature cataracts cause secondary glaucoma and permanent blindness
Do NOT use human eye drops — Visine and similar products contain vasoconstrictors harmful to dogs

cause: Direct contact with an irritating or allergenic substance — grass, cleaning products, floor detergents, fertilizers, certain plants, rubber, plastic food bowls, or carpet chemicals. Typically appears on the belly, paws, and face which contact the ground most.
warning: If rash spreads rapidly, dog is in severe distress, or there is facial swelling — this may be an allergic reaction, not simple contact dermatitis. See vet immediately.

medicines.name: Hydrocortisone Cream 1% (OTC)
medicines.dose: Apply thin layer to affected area 2 times daily for up to 5 days
medicines.note: Use E-collar to prevent licking. Do not use on broken skin or for more than 7 days.

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Spray (Antibacterial + Soothing)
topical_treatments.use: Spray on rash after washing the area. Prevents secondary bacterial infection from scratching. Allow to dry naturally.

topical_treatments.name: Aloe Vera Gel (Pure, no additives)
topical_treatments.use: Apply a thin layer to the rash to soothe irritation and redness. Only pure aloe — NOT products containing alcohol, xylitol, or added fragrance.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Skin Barrier Repair)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds the skin's natural lipid barrier that was breached by the irritant. Reduces inflammatory redness and itch from inside.
supplements.dose: Daily with food during and after the rash heals

safety_warnings: Identify and remove the trigger — rash will keep returning if the dog keeps contacting the irritant
Switch from plastic food bowls to stainless steel — plastic causes chin rash in many dogs
Rinse paws after walks in areas that may have been treated with pesticides or fertilizers
NEVER use human cortisone cream with concentrations above 1% — higher strengths cause skin thinning in dogs

cause: Bee or insect sting (most common sudden swelling), foreign body between toes (grass awn, thorn, splinter), interdigital cyst, bacterial infection, broken nail, or fracture from jumping or trauma.
warning: If swelling is spreading up the leg or the dog has a fever — this may be a spreading bacterial infection (cellulitis). See vet immediately for IV antibiotics.

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl) for insect sting
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg orally every 8 hours for 24-48 hours after sting
medicines.note: For bee or wasp sting swelling only. If face is swelling or dog is having trouble breathing — emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Cephalexin (for infected paw)
medicines.dose: 22 mg/kg twice daily for 7-14 days
medicines.note: For confirmed bacterial infection of the paw. Continue full course.

topical_treatments.name: Epsom Salt Warm Paw Soak
topical_treatments.use: 2 tbsp Epsom salt in 2 cups warm water. Soak paw 10-15 minutes twice daily. Reduces swelling, draws out infection or foreign bodies.

topical_treatments.name: Betadine (Povidone-Iodine 1%) Antiseptic Soak
topical_treatments.use: Dilute to weak tea color. Soak paw 5-10 minutes twice daily. Kills bacteria in wound or between toes.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Anti-inflammatory)
supplements.benefit: Reduces systemic inflammation that contributes to paw swelling from infection or allergic reaction
supplements.dose: Daily with food during recovery

safety_warnings: Swelling spreading up the leg + fever = cellulitis emergency. IV antibiotics needed urgently.
Always check between the toes for embedded grass awns — they migrate deep and cause abscesses
Use E-collar to prevent licking — licking massively worsens infection and swelling
Facial swelling alongside paw swelling after an insect sting = anaphylaxis emergency

aliases: dog sick after drinking outside water
cause: Puddles, ponds, and stagnant water contain Giardia, Leptospira bacteria, Cryptosporidium, blue-green algae toxins (cyanobacteria — highly toxic), and various chemical runoff. Even a small amount of blue-green algae water can cause fatal liver failure within hours.
warning: Blue-green algae (found in warm stagnant ponds) is EXTREMELY TOXIC. If dog swam in or drank from a green-blue colored pond or lake and becomes suddenly ill — this is a medical emergency. Rush to vet immediately.

medicines.name: Metronidazole (Flagyl) for Giardia
medicines.dose: 25 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days with food
medicines.note: If Giardia confirmed after drinking contaminated water. Fecal test needed for confirmation.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Restores gut flora disrupted by waterborne pathogens. Reduces duration of diarrhea from contaminated water ingestion.
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily with food for 2-4 weeks after incident

supplements.name: SAMe + Milk Thistle (Liver Protection)
supplements.benefit: If algae toxin or chemical exposure suspected, these liver protective supplements help while awaiting vet care
supplements.dose: Begin under vet guidance after any suspected algae toxin exposure

safety_warnings: Blue-green algae water is FATAL — sudden illness after pond swimming = emergency vet immediately
Always carry fresh water on walks to reduce dog's urge to drink from puddles
Vaccinate against Leptospirosis annually if dog lives near water or wildlife areas
Watch for vomiting, diarrhea, or weakness in the 24 hours after drinking unknown water

cause: Hot pavement in summer (paw pad burns), walking on salt or de-icing chemicals in cold areas, nutritional deficiency, dry weather, excessive licking, or hyperkeratosis (inherited thickening of pads in certain breeds).
warning: If paw pads are severely cracked and bleeding, or the dog is severely lame — this may be a paw pad burn from hot pavement or a chemical burn. Flush with water immediately and see vet.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (if cracks are infected)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7 days
medicines.note: Only if cracks show signs of infection: redness, swelling, pus, or dog is feverish

topical_treatments.name: Musher's Secret Paw Wax
topical_treatments.active: Blend of natural waxes and oils
topical_treatments.use: Apply thin layer to pads before walks. Protects against hot pavement, salt, and rough terrain. Also moisturises cracked pads.

topical_treatments.name: Virgin Coconut Oil (Natural Paw Moisturiser)
topical_treatments.use: Massage small amount into cracked pads after walks. Safe if dog licks it. Apply before bedtime so it absorbs overnight.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Skin + Pad Health)
supplements.benefit: Omega-3 fatty acids moisturize paw pads from the inside by improving the skin's natural oil production and barrier function
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7) for Pad Repair
supplements.benefit: Biotin is essential for keratin production in paw pads. Speeds healing of cracked and thickened pads.
supplements.dose: Once daily with food for 6-8 weeks minimum

safety_warnings: Test pavement heat with your hand for 5 seconds before walking — if too hot for you, too hot for paw pads
NEVER use human hand creams or Vaseline long-term on pads — they soften pads too much making them vulnerable
After walks on chemically treated roads rinse paws with water immediately
Walk dogs in early morning or evening in summer to avoid hot pavement burns

common_name: Dog Biting or Chewing Its Own Tail
cause: Flea allergy dermatitis (most common — fleas concentrate at tail base), anal gland impaction, tail injury or fracture pain, hot spot at tail base, intestinal worms, or obsessive compulsive disorder (spinning/tail chasing).
warning: If the dog has chewed through the skin and there is a bleeding wound on the tail — this can become severely infected or require amputation if not treated. See vet same day.

medicines.name: Frontline Plus (Fipronil) Spot-on for Flea Control
medicines.dose: Monthly spot-on between shoulder blades
medicines.note: Flea allergy at tail base is the most common cause. Treat even if you cannot see fleas — one flea bite triggers the reaction.

topical_treatments.name: Chlorhexidine 2% Spray (Wound prevention)
topical_treatments.use: Spray on bitten tail area to prevent infection. Allow to dry. Use twice daily.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply to bitten area after antiseptic spray to promote healing and prevent secondary infection

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Flea Allergy Skin Repair)
supplements.benefit: Reduces the hypersensitive skin response to flea bites that causes tail base itching. Repairs skin barrier.
supplements.dose: Daily with food throughout flea season and beyond

safety_warnings: Use E-collar immediately if skin is broken — infection from a self-inflicted tail wound spreads fast
Deworm the dog — tapeworms and roundworms cause anal/tail base irritation triggering biting
Open wound on tail needs same-day vet visit — tail wounds are prone to serious infection
Obsessive spinning or tail chasing that cannot be interrupted may indicate OCD or neurological issue

cause: Lack of regular nail trimming. Dogs that walk on soft surfaces (grass, carpet) do not naturally wear down nails. Overgrown nails change the dog's gait, cause joint pain over time, and can curl into the paw pad causing severe pain and infection.
warning: A nail that has curled completely and grown into the paw pad is a medical emergency — causes extreme pain, infection, and requires vet removal under sedation.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (if nail grows into pad)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7 days after removal
medicines.note: Only if the nail has pierced the pad and caused infection. Vet removal needed first.

topical_treatments.name: Styptic Powder (Kwik Stop) for Nail Bleeding
topical_treatments.use: If you accidentally cut the quick (pink blood vessel in nail) — apply styptic powder directly to the bleeding nail end and apply gentle pressure for 30 seconds.

supplements.name: Biotin (Vitamin B7) for Strong Nails
supplements.benefit: Biotin strengthens nail keratin, making nails less brittle and less prone to splitting during trimming
supplements.dose: Once daily with food. Also improves coat and paw pad quality.

safety_warnings: Nail curled into pad = vet emergency — causes extreme pain and serious infection
Trim a tiny amount every 2 weeks rather than large amounts monthly — keeps the quick short
If you cut the quick and bleeding does not stop in 5 minutes — go to vet
Long nails change the dog's posture and cause joint pain and back problems over time

cause: Overflow of tears due to blocked or absent tear ducts, shallow eye socket (brachycephalic breeds), allergies, entropion (eyelid rolling inward), or chronic irritation. The wet fur around the eyes gets stained reddish-brown by a pigment (porphyrin) in tears.
warning: Yellow or green eye discharge = infection, not simple tearing. If the eye itself looks red or the dog is squinting — see vet. May be conjunctivitis, corneal ulcer, or glaucoma.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops (only if infection confirmed)
medicines.dose: 1-2 drops 3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: For bacterial conjunctivitis with discharge. Clear watery tearing without redness does not need antibiotic drops.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye Wipes
topical_treatments.use: Gently wipe eye discharge and stained fur daily with a soft damp cotton pad. Use separate pad for each eye. Keeps skin clean and prevents secondary bacterial growth in wet fur.

topical_treatments.name: Angel Eyes Tear Stain Remover Wipes
topical_treatments.use: Wipe stained fur around eyes daily. Removes porphyrin staining gradually over 2-4 weeks of consistent use.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Tear Film + Allergy)
supplements.benefit: Improves tear film quality reducing overflow tearing. Reduces allergy-driven excessive tearing in sensitive breeds.
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: NEVER use human Visine or any vasoconstrictor eye drops — harmful to dogs
Yellow or green discharge = infection. Watery clear discharge = tearing. They are different.
Brachycephalic breeds (Pugs, Shih Tzus, Maltese) have structural tearing — manage, not cure
Keep fur trimmed away from eyes — fur touching the eye causes constant irritation and more tearing

cause: Alert barking (territorial), boredom or insufficient exercise, separation anxiety, attention-seeking, cognitive dysfunction in older dogs, pain (dog cannot communicate pain verbally), or reaction to other animals.
warning: Sudden onset excessive barking in an older dog that previously did not bark much — rule out pain (arthritis, dental disease) or canine cognitive dysfunction. Dogs bark when they hurt.

medicines.name: Fluoxetine (Reconcile) for anxiety-driven barking
medicines.dose: 1-2 mg/kg once daily. Takes 4-6 weeks for full effect.
medicines.note: Only for anxiety-driven barking. Must be combined with behavioral training. Not a standalone fix.

topical_treatments.name: Adaptil DAP Diffuser
topical_treatments.use: Plug into main room. Reduces anxiety-driven barking by 30-50%. Replace refill monthly.

supplements.name: Zesty Paws Calming Bites (L-Theanine + Melatonin)
supplements.benefit: Reduces the anxiety and hyperarousal that drives excessive barking. Safe for daily use without sedation.
supplements.dose: As directed on packaging by weight. Give 30-60 minutes before high-stimulus situations.

safety_warnings: NEVER use shock or citronella bark collars — cause pain and fear, worsen anxiety-driven barking
NEVER shout at a barking dog — the dog thinks you are barking along and gets more excited
Sudden barking in an older dog that was quiet before — check for pain or cognitive dysfunction
Sufficient exercise (2 walks daily minimum) resolves boredom-driven barking in most cases

cause: Inner ear imbalance from movement conflicting with what the dog sees. More common in puppies whose inner ear is still developing. Can also be anxiety-based (negative association with car = vet visits). Usually improves with age and positive exposure.
warning: Excessive panting, drooling, and restlessness in the car followed by vomiting is classic motion sickness. If the dog is also extremely fearful or panicking — anxiety medication may be needed before travel.

medicines.name: Maropitant (Cerenia) — Best anti-nausea for dogs
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg orally 2 hours before travel. Can be given for up to 2 consecutive days.
medicines.note: Most effective anti-nausea medication for dogs. Also reduces anxiety-related nausea component.

medicines.name: Meclizine (OTC Motion Sickness)
medicines.dose: 25 mg per medium/large dog 1 hour before travel
medicines.note: OTC alternative to Cerenia. Less effective but useful for occasional mild car sickness.

topical_treatments.name: Adaptil DAP Spray for Travel
topical_treatments.use: Spray car interior and dog's blanket 15 minutes before travel. Reduces anxiety component of car sickness.

supplements.name: Ginger (Natural Anti-nausea)
supplements.benefit: Ginger has proven anti-nausea properties. Safe for dogs and reduces the nausea component of motion sickness naturally.
supplements.dose: 25 mg per 5 kg body weight given 30 minutes before travel. Plain ginger capsules or ginger biscuits.

safety_warnings: Do NOT feed dog 2-3 hours before car travel — an empty stomach reduces nausea significantly
Ensure good ventilation in the car — heat worsens nausea dramatically
Face the dog forward in the car — side-facing and backward movement worsens inner ear imbalance
Build positive car associations — short trips to parks, not just to the vet

cause: Overproduction of cerumen (ear wax) due to allergies (most common), floppy ears trapping moisture, ear mites, or yeast overgrowth. Dark brown to black waxy buildup with or without odor. Different from an ear infection — wax buildup without inflammation or smell is simple ceruminosis.
warning: If the wax is accompanied by a bad odor, redness inside the ear, or the dog is shaking its head and scratching — this is an ear infection, not just wax. See vet for ear drops.

medicines.name: No medicine needed for simple wax buildup
medicines.dose: Regular cleaning with proper ear cleaner is all that is needed for uncomplicated wax buildup
medicines.note: If smell or pain is present — this is an infection and needs Otomax or similar antibiotic ear drops

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Epi-Otic Ear Cleanser
topical_treatments.use: Fill ear canal, massage base of ear 30 seconds, let dog shake, wipe outer ear with cotton. Use once weekly for maintenance. Do NOT use cotton swabs deep in the canal.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Allergy Control)
supplements.benefit: Allergies are the #1 cause of excessive ear wax production. Omega-3 reduces the allergic response that stimulates overproduction of cerumen.
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term for dogs with recurring wax issues

safety_warnings: NEVER insert cotton swabs into the ear canal — pushes wax deeper and risks eardrum damage
Clean ears after every bath and swim — trapped moisture turns wax into infection
Foul smell from ear = infection, not just wax. Needs antibiotic ear drops, not just cleaning.
Never use hydrogen peroxide or alcohol in dog ears — causes burning and damages ear tissue

aliases: dog not using leg after shot
cause: Mild temporary inflammation at the injection site causing soreness and reluctance to use that leg. Usually occurs within 12-24 hours of vaccination and resolves in 1-3 days. The body mounting an immune response also causes mild general tiredness.
warning: If limping is severe, the dog has facial swelling, difficulty breathing, vomiting, or collapse after vaccination — this is a vaccine anaphylaxis reaction. Rush to vet immediately.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) — if needed for pain
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once with food if pain appears significant
medicines.note: Usually not needed — most post-vaccine soreness resolves on its own in 24-48 hours. Ask the vet who gave the vaccine before giving any medication.

topical_treatments.name: Cool Compress on Injection Site
topical_treatments.use: Apply cool (not ice cold) damp cloth to injection site for 5-10 minutes twice daily for first 24 hours. Reduces local inflammation and soreness.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (General Anti-inflammatory)
supplements.benefit: Reduces the local and systemic inflammatory response after vaccination, speeding recovery from injection site soreness
supplements.dose: Daily with food — give on day of vaccination and for a few days after

safety_warnings: Facial swelling + vomiting + collapse after vaccination = anaphylaxis. Rush to vet immediately.
Limping lasting more than 3 days after vaccination = see vet to rule out injection site reaction
A lump at injection site that grows larger over weeks = see vet urgently. Rare injection site sarcoma possible.
Never withhold future vaccines due to mild soreness — the disease risk is far greater than vaccine soreness

cause: Bordetella bronchiseptica bacteria alone or combined with canine parainfluenza virus. Spreads through the air in kennels, dog parks, grooming salons, and vet waiting rooms. The dog sounds like a goose honking.
warning: If the dog develops fever, stops eating, or cough lasts more than 3 weeks — see vet immediately. May have progressed to pneumonia which is life-threatening.

medicines.name: Doxycycline
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days with food
medicines.note: For confirmed bacterial cases. Give with food to prevent nausea.

topical_treatments.name: Warm Steam Inhalation
topical_treatments.use: Run hot shower, close bathroom, sit with dog in steam 10 minutes twice daily. Loosens mucus and soothes the irritated trachea.

supplements.name: Manuka Honey
supplements.benefit: Soothes throat lining, mild antibacterial properties, reduces cough irritation naturally
supplements.dose: 0.5 tsp small dog / 1 tsp large dog twice daily. Do NOT give to puppies under 1 year.

supplements.name: Vitamin C Immune Support
supplements.benefit: Supports immune response and speeds recovery from respiratory infection
supplements.dose: 125 mg per 5 kg body weight daily during illness

safety_warnings: Isolate from other dogs for 2 weeks — kennel cough is airborne and extremely contagious
Use a harness instead of neck collar — collar pressure on trachea worsens coughing
Vaccinate annually with Bordetella vaccine for dogs visiting kennels or dog parks
If cough lasts more than 3 weeks or dog develops fever — see vet for pneumonia check

cause: Very common in puppies due to diet changes (new food, new home), stress, intestinal worms, Parvovirus, Giardia, Coccidia, or bacterial overgrowth. Puppies dehydrate much faster than adult dogs making even mild diarrhea more dangerous.
warning: Puppies can become dangerously dehydrated within hours of diarrhea. Any blood in stool, vomiting alongside diarrhea, or extreme lethargy in a puppy = emergency vet visit immediately.

medicines.name: Metronidazole (Flagyl)
medicines.dose: 10-15 mg/kg twice daily for 5-7 days with food
medicines.note: Safe in puppies. Covers Giardia, Coccidia, and Clostridium — common puppy diarrhea causes.

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Specifically formulated and proven to shorten puppy diarrhea episodes by restoring healthy gut bacteria
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily sprinkled on food. Safe from 6 weeks of age.

supplements.name: ORS / Electral Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces fluids and electrolytes lost through diarrhea. Critical in puppies who dehydrate very quickly.
supplements.dose: Offer freely as drinking water during illness. A puppy refusing water with diarrhea needs IV fluids at the vet.

safety_warnings: Blood in puppy stool = emergency vet immediately — Parvovirus kills puppies within 24-48 hours
Puppies dehydrate much faster than adult dogs — treat diarrhea seriously from the first episode
Deworm all puppies — worms are the most common cause of puppy diarrhea
NEVER give Imodium to puppies — causes dangerous neurological side effects

cause: Completely normal and harmless in puppies — caused by excitement, eating too fast, or swallowing air. Occasional hiccups in adult dogs are also normal. Persistent hiccups lasting more than 1 hour, or accompanied by retching, coughing, or weight loss, may indicate acid reflux or gastrointestinal disease.
warning: Hiccups lasting more than 1 hour continuously in an adult dog = see vet. May indicate acid reflux, diaphragm irritation, or respiratory disease.

medicines.name: No medicine needed for normal hiccups
medicines.dose: Almost always resolves on its own within minutes. Persistent cases may need Famotidine for acid reflux.
medicines.note: Famotidine 0.5 mg/kg once daily if hiccups are linked to acid reflux after meals

supplements.name: Purina Fortiflora Probiotic
supplements.benefit: Helps regulate gut motility and reduces gas production that can contribute to hiccuping in dogs with sensitive stomachs
supplements.dose: 1 sachet daily with food for dogs with recurring hiccups after meals

safety_warnings: Puppy hiccups are completely normal — no treatment needed
Slow down fast eaters using a slow-feeder bowl — eating fast is the most common cause
Hiccups lasting more than 1 hour = see vet to rule out acid reflux or diaphragm issue
Never try to frighten or startle the dog to stop hiccups — causes unnecessary stress

cause: Trichodectes canis (biting louse) or Linognathus setosus (sucking louse) infestation. Spread through direct contact with infected dogs or contaminated grooming tools. Unlike fleas, dog lice are species-specific — they cannot infect humans.
warning: Dog lice cannot infect humans. However, they spread easily between dogs through shared bedding, grooming tools, and direct contact. Treat all dogs in the household simultaneously.

medicines.name: Fipronil Spot-On (Frontline Plus)
medicines.dose: Monthly spot-on applied between shoulder blades
medicines.note: Highly effective against dog lice. Repeat application in 4 weeks to kill newly hatched lice.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Erina-EP Tick and Flea Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Lather and leave on for 10 minutes before rinsing. Use weekly during infestation. Also kills lice on contact.

topical_treatments.name: Fine-tooth Lice Comb
topical_treatments.use: Comb through the coat section by section over a white paper towel to remove lice and nits. Do daily during treatment.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Soothes skin irritation from lice bites and rebuilds coat health after infestation
supplements.dose: Daily with food during and after treatment
