safety_warnings: Dog lice cannot infect humans — no need to treat family members
Disinfect all grooming tools, collars, and bedding — lice eggs survive on objects
Treat all dogs in the household simultaneously even if only one shows symptoms
Repeat spot-on treatment in 4 weeks — the first treatment does not kill unhatched eggs

common_name: Weak or Wobbly Back Legs (Hind Limb Weakness)
cause: Common causes: IVDD (slipped disc — most common sudden onset), degenerative myelopathy (progressive nerve disease in older dogs), hip dysplasia, spinal tumor, fibrocartilaginous embolism (spinal stroke), or severe arthritis.
warning: Sudden hind leg weakness that appears over hours = EMERGENCY. Spinal cord damage becomes permanent after 24-48 hours. Do not wait to see if it improves on its own.

medicines.name: Gabapentin + Methylprednisolone (acute disc compression)
medicines.dose: Per vet protocol. Methylprednisolone given IV at vet clinic for acute spinal compression.
medicines.note: Treatment depends entirely on the underlying diagnosis — imaging (MRI/CT) is needed first.

supplements.name: Methylcobalamin B12 (Nerve Regeneration)
supplements.benefit: Essential for myelin sheath repair and nerve fiber regeneration in spinal and peripheral nerve conditions
supplements.dose: Under vet guidance during recovery phase. Also given IV at vet clinic in acute phase.

supplements.name: Cosequin DS (Glucosamine + Chondroitin)
supplements.benefit: For arthritis or hip dysplasia causing hind weakness — supports joint cartilage and reduces pain contributing to weakness
supplements.dose: Double loading dose for first 4-6 weeks

safety_warnings: Sudden hind leg weakness = EMERGENCY. Rush to vet — spinal cord death is permanent after 24-48 hours
Never give human NSAIDs — ibuprofen and paracetamol are fatal to dogs
Support the dog's hind end when moving — do not let them drag themselves on hard floors
Physical rehabilitation after treatment dramatically improves recovery outcomes

cause: Overgrowth of Malassezia pachydermatis yeast that normally lives on dog skin. Triggered by allergies (most common), hypothyroidism, Cushing's disease, or moisture in skin folds. The skin has a distinctive musty or corn-chip smell.
warning: Yeast dermatitis almost always has an underlying cause — allergies, hormones, or immune issues. Treating only the yeast without addressing the root cause leads to rapid relapse.

medicines.name: Ketoconazole Oral Tablet
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg once daily with food for 2-4 weeks
medicines.note: For widespread or severe yeast infection. Monitor liver enzymes with long-term use.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Pyohex Shampoo (Chlorhexidine 3% + Miconazole 1%)
topical_treatments.use: Leave on 5-10 minutes before rinsing. Use twice weekly. Most effective topical treatment for Malassezia.

topical_treatments.name: Nizoral Shampoo (Ketoconazole 2%)
topical_treatments.use: Apply to affected areas, leave 5 minutes, rinse. Use twice weekly alongside Pyohex or as alternative.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Skin Barrier Repair)
supplements.benefit: Rebuilds the defective skin barrier that allows yeast overgrowth. Reduces allergy-driven inflammation that feeds yeast proliferation.
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term — must treat the underlying allergy cause

safety_warnings: Musty or corn-chip smell from skin = likely yeast. Regular soap will not treat it — use antifungal shampoo.
Skin folds (Bulldogs, Shar Peis) need daily cleaning and drying — trapped moisture grows yeast rapidly
Recurrent yeast means an underlying trigger — rule out allergies, hypothyroidism, or Cushing's
Never use human athlete's foot cream on dog skin without vet guidance — many contain harmful additives

cause: Repeated pressure on the elbow from lying on hard floors causes the skin to thicken and form a callus. Common in large and giant breeds. If a fluid-filled sac develops under the callus, this is a hygroma — a more serious condition.
warning: If the callus cracks and bleeds, or a soft fluid-filled lump develops under the skin (hygroma), this requires vet treatment. Infected hygromas can require surgery.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (if callus becomes infected)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days
medicines.note: Only if the cracked callus shows signs of infection: redness, warmth, pus discharge.

topical_treatments.name: Musher's Secret Paw and Skin Wax
topical_treatments.use: Apply a thin layer to the callus daily. Softens the thickened skin and prevents cracking and bleeding.

topical_treatments.name: Virgin Coconut Oil
topical_treatments.use: Massage into callus daily. Softens thickened skin and reduces cracking. Safe if dog licks it.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 + Biotin
supplements.benefit: Omega-3 improves skin hydration from inside reducing callus severity. Biotin supports healthy skin cell turnover.
supplements.dose: Both daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: Soft fluid-filled swelling under the elbow = hygroma, not just callus. See vet — can get infected.
Provide orthopedic foam bedding — this is the single most effective prevention and treatment
Never lance or drain a hygroma at home — causes serious infection
Cracked bleeding callus needs antiseptic and vet attention to prevent bone infection (osteomyelitis)

cause: Normal in mother dogs with puppies. In adult dogs: nutritional deficiency (especially enzyme or B-vitamin deficiency), boredom, attention-seeking, learned behavior, or medical conditions causing increased appetite (Cushing's, diabetes, parasites, malabsorption).
warning: Eating other dogs' or wild animal feces carries real disease risk — Parvovirus, worms, Giardia, Leptospirosis, and Salmonella can all be transmitted through fecal ingestion.

medicines.name: For-Bid (Glutamic acid deterrent)
medicines.dose: Sprinkle on the dog's food — makes feces taste bitter and unpalatable to them
medicines.note: Works for some dogs. Also sprinkle on feces directly if the dog eats other dogs' poop.

supplements.name: Digestive Enzyme Supplement + Purina Fortiflora
supplements.benefit: Many dogs eat poop due to enzyme or nutrient deficiency — they seek undigested nutrients in feces. Enzymes improve food absorption reducing this drive.
supplements.dose: Enzyme supplement with every meal. Fortiflora 1 sachet daily.

supplements.name: B-Vitamin Complex
supplements.benefit: B-vitamin deficiency is a documented cause of coprophagia. Supplementing often resolves the behavior.
supplements.dose: Once daily with food. Results seen in 2-4 weeks if deficiency was the cause.

safety_warnings: Eating other animals' feces transmits Parvovirus, worms, Giardia, and Leptospirosis — serious health risk
Deworm the dog and run a full fecal test if this behavior is new
Pick up feces immediately on walks — remove the opportunity as the most effective prevention
Sudden onset coprophagia in an adult dog that never did this before = rule out diabetes or Cushing's

cause: Most common in toy breed puppies (Chihuahuas, Yorkshire Terriers, Maltese) that go too long without eating. Also in diabetic dogs given too much insulin, hunting dogs after extreme exertion, or dogs with insulinoma (insulin-secreting tumor).
warning: THIS IS AN EMERGENCY. Untreated hypoglycemia causes brain damage and death within minutes. If the dog is conscious — give corn syrup or honey on gums immediately and rush to vet.

medicines.name: Dextrose 50% IV (Emergency — Vet Only)
medicines.dose: 0.5 ml/kg IV diluted 1:4 given slowly by vet
medicines.note: For unconscious or severely affected dogs. Oral glucose first if dog is conscious.

topical_treatments.name: Karo Corn Syrup or Honey (Emergency First Aid)
topical_treatments.use: If dog is conscious and can swallow — rub a small amount of corn syrup or honey on the gums immediately. Do NOT give to an unconscious dog — risk of choking. Then rush to vet.

supplements.name: NutriCal High-Calorie Paste (Prevention for toy breeds)
supplements.benefit: High-calorie glucose supplement that prevents hypoglycemic episodes in toy breed puppies when given between meals or before exercise
supplements.dose: Small amount on finger every 4-6 hours for toy breed puppies. Always feed 3-4 small meals daily.

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rub honey or corn syrup on gums if conscious, then rush to vet immediately
NEVER give sugar water or food to an unconscious dog — aspiration risk
Toy breed puppies must eat every 4-6 hours — missing meals causes hypoglycemia
Diabetic dog that received insulin but did not eat = hypoglycemia emergency

cause: Occasional sneezing: completely normal. Frequent sneezing: allergies (pollen, dust), foreign body in nose (grass seed, dust), nasal mites, nasal infection, or a playful communication signal during play (play sneezing).
warning: Blood from only ONE nostril with persistent sneezing in a middle-aged or older dog = possible nasal tumor or Aspergillus fungal infection. See vet urgently.

medicines.name: Cetirizine (Zyrtec) for allergy sneezing
medicines.dose: 0.5 mg/kg once daily. Plain Cetirizine only — NOT formulations with decongestants.
medicines.note: For confirmed allergy-triggered sneezing. Safe for most dogs.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Nasal Rinse
topical_treatments.use: A few drops in each nostril to flush irritants and debris. Tilt head back slightly. 2-3 times daily during heavy pollen season.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Allergy Control)
supplements.benefit: Reduces nasal mucosal inflammation from allergies, decreasing frequency of allergy-triggered sneezing episodes
supplements.dose: Daily with food during and after pollen season

safety_warnings: Blood from one nostril in middle-aged or older dog = urgent vet visit — possible tumor
NEVER use human nasal sprays (Otrivin, Nasivion) — xylometazoline is toxic to dogs
A grass seed or foxtail lodged in the nose causes intense violent sneezing — vet removal needed
Sneezing during play is normal dog communication — not a health concern

cause: Most common: bee or wasp sting, tooth root abscess (most common cause of one-sided cheek swelling), allergic reaction, snake bite, or abscess from a bite wound. The cause determines urgency — allergic swelling can progress to anaphylaxis.
warning: Facial swelling with breathing difficulty, pale gums, or collapse = ANAPHYLAXIS EMERGENCY. Rush to vet immediately. Swelling from a tooth abscess also needs same-day vet attention.

medicines.name: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl) for mild allergic swelling
medicines.dose: 1 mg/kg orally every 8 hours. Plain Benadryl only — no decongestant formulations.
medicines.note: ONLY for mild hives or face swelling with NO breathing difficulty. Any breathing issue = vet immediately.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (for tooth abscess)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days alongside dental treatment
medicines.note: Antibiotics alone do not cure a dental abscess — the tooth needs extraction or root canal.

topical_treatments.name: Cold Compress (for sting or bite swelling)
topical_treatments.use: Apply cold (not ice) damp cloth to swollen area for 10 minutes. Reduces local histamine and swelling.

supplements.name: Quercetin (Natural Antihistamine — Preventative)
supplements.benefit: Stabilizes mast cells reducing severity of future allergic reactions in dogs prone to bee sting or food allergy facial swelling
supplements.dose: 8 mg per kg twice daily with food as daily preventative. Not for acute emergency.

safety_warnings: Breathing difficulty + face swelling = ANAPHYLAXIS. Rush to emergency vet immediately.
One-sided cheek swelling below the eye = likely tooth root abscess. Needs dental treatment, not just antibiotics.
NEVER give Benadryl-D — the decongestant pseudoephedrine causes fatal heart arrhythmia in dogs
Snake bite swelling grows rapidly and needs anti-venom at vet — do not wait at home

cause: Demodex mites passed from mother to puppy during nursing. Almost all puppies carry some Demodex but only develop mange when immunity is low. Localized (small patches) usually self-resolves. Generalized (spreading) indicates immune problems.
warning: Generalized Demodectic mange spreading across the body indicates the puppy has an immune deficiency. Aggressive treatment and immune support needed. Do not breed this puppy.

medicines.name: Fluralaner (Bravecto) Chewable
medicines.dose: Single oral dose by weight. Repeat in 12 weeks. Usually 2-3 doses for full resolution.
medicines.note: Safe from 8 weeks of age and 2 kg body weight. Most effective modern treatment.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Pyohex Shampoo
topical_treatments.use: Treats secondary bacterial and yeast infections in damaged follicles. Use twice weekly.

supplements.name: Beaphar Multivitamine (Immune Booster for Puppy)
supplements.benefit: Strengthens puppy immune system to naturally suppress Demodex overpopulation. Critical — mites only proliferate when immunity is weak.
supplements.dose: Daily with food throughout treatment period

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Supports skin barrier repair and immune regulation during treatment
supplements.dose: Daily with food

safety_warnings: Demodectic mange is NOT contagious to humans or other adult dogs
Localized puppy mange (1-2 small patches) often resolves on its own — watch for 4-6 weeks
Generalized spreading mange needs aggressive treatment and immune support immediately
Do not breed puppies or dogs with generalized Demodex — indicates inherited immune weakness

cause: Chewing on very hard objects — bones, antlers, rocks, hard nylon toys. Falls or trauma to the mouth. The pulp (nerve inside the tooth) becomes exposed in a broken tooth causing severe pain and bacterial infection of the tooth root.
warning: A broken tooth with exposed pink pulp is extremely painful and will develop a root abscess within weeks if untreated. This is not cosmetic — it is a painful medical condition requiring prompt dental treatment.

medicines.name: Meloxicam (Metacam) NSAID — Pain relief
medicines.dose: 0.1 mg/kg once daily with food
medicines.note: Pain relief until dental treatment. NEVER give human NSAIDs — ibuprofen and paracetamol are fatal.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (if abscess developing)
medicines.dose: 12.5 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days
medicines.note: Treats secondary bacterial infection. Does not cure — tooth needs extraction or root canal.

supplements.name: Calcium + Vitamin D3 (Tooth and Bone Strength)
supplements.benefit: Supports remaining tooth enamel and jaw bone density. Helps prevent further tooth fractures.
supplements.dose: As per body weight. Do not over-supplement calcium in large breed puppies.

safety_warnings: Exposed pink pulp in a broken tooth = severe pain and guaranteed infection. Needs dental treatment urgently.
NEVER give hard bones, antlers, or hard nylon toys — these are the most common causes of tooth fractures
The rule: if you cannot indent the chew toy with your thumbnail, it is too hard for dog teeth
NEVER give human NSAIDs for dental pain — ibuprofen and paracetamol cause fatal organ damage

cause: Hormonal territorial marking — most common in intact (unneutered) male dogs but also occurs in females. Also caused by anxiety, new pet in household, changes in routine, or urinary tract infection (UTI) in dogs that squat to urinate.
warning: If marking behavior is sudden in a previously housetrained dog — rule out UTI or urinary stones with a vet urine test first. Medical causes must be excluded before behavioral treatment.

medicines.name: Neutering (most effective long-term solution)
medicines.dose: Single procedure — reduces or eliminates hormonal marking in 80% of male dogs when done before 1 year old
medicines.note: Most effective intervention. Also prevents testicular cancer and prostate disease.

topical_treatments.name: Enzymatic Urine Cleaner (Nature's Miracle)
topical_treatments.use: Apply liberally to marked area, leave 10 minutes, blot dry. The enzymes break down urine proteins completely eliminating the scent that draws dogs to re-mark the same spot.

supplements.name: Zesty Paws Calming Bites (for anxiety-driven marking)
supplements.benefit: If marking is anxiety-driven (new pet, changes at home) — calming L-Theanine + Melatonin reduces the anxiety triggering the marking behavior
supplements.dose: As directed on packaging. Give 30-60 minutes before high-anxiety situations.

safety_warnings: Rule out UTI with urine test before assuming it is purely behavioral
NEVER punish a dog after the fact — they cannot connect punishment to something done minutes ago
Regular bleach does NOT eliminate urine odor — use enzymatic cleaner or the dog will keep marking the same spot
Neutering is the single most effective intervention for hormonal marking

cause: Small amounts of clear or slightly white mucus in the corner of the eye after sleep is completely normal — this is dried tears and is called 'sleep' or 'eye boogers'. Yellow or green thick discharge indicates bacterial infection. Watery overflow discharge indicates blocked tear ducts or allergies.
warning: Yellow or green discharge = bacterial conjunctivitis. Red eye + discharge + squinting = possible corneal ulcer. These need vet attention within 24-48 hours.

medicines.name: Tobramycin Eye Drops (for bacterial discharge)
medicines.dose: 1-2 drops in affected eye 3 times daily for 5-7 days
medicines.note: For yellow or green discharge indicating bacterial infection. Clear discharge usually needs only cleaning.

topical_treatments.name: Sterile Saline Eye Wipes
topical_treatments.use: Gently wipe eye discharge with soft cotton pad dampened with saline. Use separate pad for each eye. Do daily in dogs prone to discharge.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Improves tear film quality and reduces allergy-driven eye discharge in sensitive breeds
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: Small clear morning eye crust is normal — only worry if discharge is yellow, green, or continuous
NEVER use human Visine or any vasoconstrictor eye drops — harmful to dogs
Red eye + squinting + discharge = possible corneal ulcer — see vet within 24 hours
Use a separate cotton pad for each eye to prevent spreading infection between eyes

cause: The #1 cause is environmental or food allergies — paws turn red and itchy when they contact allergens (grass, cleaning products, food proteins). Other causes: interdigital cysts, foreign body (grass awn), anxiety, boredom, or yeast infection between toes.
warning: Chronic paw licking causes secondary yeast (Malassezia) infection between the toes — recognizable by a corn-chip or musty smell. This needs antifungal shampoo treatment in addition to addressing the root cause.

medicines.name: Oclacitinib (Apoquel) for allergy-driven licking
medicines.dose: 0.4-0.6 mg/kg twice daily for 14 days, then once daily
medicines.note: Most effective for allergy-driven paw licking. Works within 4 hours.

topical_treatments.name: Betadine (Povidone-Iodine 1%) Paw Soak
topical_treatments.use: Dilute to weak tea color. Soak paws 5 minutes after walks. Removes allergens and kills yeast between toes.

topical_treatments.name: Virbac Allermyl Shampoo (for paw washes)
topical_treatments.use: Use as paw wash after walks to remove environmental allergens. Leave 3 minutes, rinse, dry thoroughly.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow High-Dose Omega-3
supplements.benefit: Reduces the allergic skin response that makes paws itchy. One of the most impactful supplements for allergy-driven paw licking.
supplements.dose: High dose by weight daily. Results in 4-6 weeks of consistent use.

safety_warnings: Corn-chip smell from licked paws = secondary yeast infection. Needs antifungal shampoo treatment.
Wipe and dry paws thoroughly after every walk — this alone significantly reduces allergy-driven licking
Check between toes for embedded grass awns or thorns — these cause intense focused licking
Use E-collar if skin between toes is broken — licking an open wound causes severe infection

cause: Pain is the most common overlooked cause of sudden aggression — a dog that snaps when touched may be in pain. Other causes: fear, resource guarding, redirected aggression, hormonal (intact males), neurological disease, or idiopathic (rage syndrome in certain breeds).
warning: Sudden onset aggression in a previously gentle dog that was not aggressive before = rule out pain first. Ear infection, dental abscess, arthritis, or neurological disease all cause pain-driven aggression. Vet exam before behavioral intervention.

medicines.name: Full vet examination + pain assessment required first
medicines.dose: Complete physical exam, blood tests, and neurological assessment to rule out pain or disease before behavioral treatment
medicines.note: Pain-driven aggression resolves when pain is treated. Behavioral aggression needs a certified veterinary behaviorist.

topical_treatments.name: Adaptil DAP Diffuser (anxiety-driven aggression)
topical_treatments.use: Plug into main room. Reduces anxiety and fear-driven aggression. Most helpful for fear-based or anxiety-based reactive dogs.

supplements.name: Zesty Paws Calming Bites (L-Theanine + Melatonin)
supplements.benefit: Reduces anxiety and arousal that triggers fear-based or anxiety-based aggression. Not effective for pain-driven or dominance aggression.
supplements.dose: As directed. Give 30-60 minutes before high-trigger situations.

safety_warnings: Never punish aggression — punishment causes fear which worsens aggression and increases bite risk
Sudden aggression in a previously gentle dog = vet exam for pain first. Do not assume it is behavioral.
Consult a certified veterinary behaviorist — not a trainer — for serious aggression
Protect family members especially children — manage the environment while working on treatment

cause: Vomiting, diarrhea, heatstroke, fever, or insufficient water access. Dogs need approximately 50 ml of water per kg body weight per day. Dehydration as low as 5% causes lethargy and sunken eyes. At 10% it becomes life-threatening.
warning: Test for dehydration: gently pinch the skin on the scruff of the neck and release. In a well-hydrated dog, skin snaps back instantly. If skin stays tented for more than 2 seconds = moderate dehydration. Sunken eyes + tented skin = emergency vet for IV fluids.

medicines.name: IV Normal Saline / Ringer's Lactate (Vet Only)
medicines.dose: Calculated by vet based on degree of dehydration and body weight
medicines.note: Moderate to severe dehydration cannot be treated with oral fluids alone — IV fluids needed at vet.

supplements.name: Electral / ORS Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Replaces sodium, potassium, and glucose lost through illness. Encourages drinking and speeds rehydration in mildly dehydrated dogs.
supplements.dose: Offer as drinking water during illness. Small amounts frequently. Do NOT force feed to vomiting dogs.

safety_warnings: Skin tenting more than 2 seconds + sunken eyes = emergency vet for IV fluids immediately
Always provide fresh water in multiple locations — especially in hot weather and after exercise
Vomiting dogs cannot rehydrate orally — IV fluids at vet are essential
Small dogs and puppies dehydrate much faster than large adult dogs — act faster with them

common_name: Snoring or Noisy Breathing (Brachycephalic Obstructive Airway Syndrome)
cause: Anatomical defects in flat-faced breeds (Bulldogs, Pugs, French Bulldogs, Shih Tzus, Boxers): stenotic nares (narrow nostrils), elongated soft palate, and hypoplastic trachea. These obstruct airflow causing noisy breathing, snoring, and inability to handle heat or exercise.
warning: Blue or gray gums during exercise or excitement = oxygen emergency. Rush to vet immediately. These dogs cannot pant efficiently and are at extreme heatstroke risk even in mild weather.

medicines.name: Corrective surgery (most effective treatment)
medicines.dose: Nares widening + soft palate shortening surgery. Best done before 2 years old for optimal results.
medicines.note: Surgery dramatically improves quality of life and reduces heatstroke risk. Not optional for severely affected dogs.

supplements.name: Himalaya Furglow Omega-3 (Airway Inflammation Reduction)
supplements.benefit: Reduces mucosal inflammation in the upper airway, slightly improving airflow and reducing the severity of respiratory noise
supplements.dose: Daily with food long-term

safety_warnings: Blue gums during exercise = oxygen emergency. Rush to vet immediately.
NEVER leave these breeds in a hot car or in the sun — they overheat and die faster than other breeds
Walk only in early morning or evening — avoid exercise in temperatures above 25°C
Keep them at lean body weight — obesity makes airway obstruction dramatically worse

cause: Allergies (most common), viral upper respiratory infection, foreign body in nose, nasal polyp, dental root infection draining into nasal cavity, or fungal infection (Aspergillosis). Dogs prefer nasal breathing — mouth breathing at rest indicates significant nasal obstruction.
warning: If the dog is breathing through its mouth at rest and appears distressed — this is significant obstruction. See vet. One-sided nasal congestion with discharge in an older dog = possible tumor or Aspergillosis.

medicines.name: Doxycycline (for bacterial rhinitis)
medicines.dose: 5-10 mg/kg twice daily for 10-14 days with food
medicines.note: For confirmed bacterial nasal infection. Culture and sensitivity testing recommended for chronic cases.

topical_treatments.name: Steam Inhalation (Congestion Relief)
topical_treatments.use: Run hot shower, close bathroom, sit with dog in steam 10-15 minutes twice daily. Loosens nasal mucus and provides relief from congestion.

supplements.name: Vitamin C + Zinc Immune Support
supplements.benefit: Supports immune function and mucosal healing during nasal infections. Reduces severity and duration of viral upper respiratory infections.
supplements.dose: 125 mg Vitamin C per 5 kg daily during congestion

safety_warnings: NEVER use human nasal decongestant drops or sprays — xylometazoline is toxic to dogs
One-sided nasal congestion with bloody discharge in older dog = urgent vet visit for tumor screening
Breathing through mouth at rest = significant obstruction — see vet
Never give antihistamines with decongestants (like Benadryl-D) — decongestant is toxic to dogs

cause: Pale or white gums indicate inadequate blood circulation or oxygen delivery — caused by internal bleeding (ruptured spleen or liver tumor), severe anemia (from hookworms, rat poison, immune disease), shock, or heart failure.
warning: Pale or white gums is ALWAYS a medical emergency. Normal gums are bubblegum pink. Press on the gum — it should return to pink within 2 seconds (capillary refill time). Longer than 2 seconds or white gums = rush to emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Emergency IV Fluids + Oxygen (Vet Emergency)
medicines.dose: IV fluid resuscitation for shock. Blood transfusion if severe anemia. Oxygen therapy.
medicines.note: The underlying cause must be found and treated — pale gums are a symptom not a diagnosis.

supplements.name: Iron + B12 Supplement (Post-anemia recovery only)
supplements.benefit: Supports red blood cell production during recovery from anemia after the emergency cause has been treated
supplements.dose: Only begin after acute cause is treated and under vet guidance. Not for emergency use.

safety_warnings: Pale or white gums = EMERGENCY. Rush to vet immediately — do not wait.
Check gum color regularly — you need to know what is normal for your dog to recognize abnormal
Capillary refill time longer than 2 seconds = shock or circulatory failure. Emergency vet.
Rat poison causes internal bleeding over 3-5 days — pale gums may appear days after ingestion

cause: Bacterial or viral infection (most common), immune-mediated disease, tick-borne illness (Ehrlichiosis, Anaplasmosis), vaccine reaction (mild, 24-48 hours post-vaccine), or rarely toxin ingestion. Normal dog temperature is 38-39.2°C. Above 39.5°C = fever.
warning: Temperature above 41°C is life-threatening and causes organ damage. Any fever combined with extreme lethargy, bloody discharge, difficulty breathing, or collapse = emergency vet immediately.

medicines.name: Dipyrone (Metamizole) — Vet-prescribed fever reducer
medicines.dose: 25 mg/kg per vet protocol
medicines.note: NEVER give human fever reducers — paracetamol causes fatal liver failure in dogs. Ibuprofen causes kidney failure.

topical_treatments.name: Tepid Water Cooling (for high fever first aid)
topical_treatments.use: Apply room-temperature (not cold) wet cloth to paws and armpits. Fan the dog. Offer small amounts of cool water. Take temperature every 15 minutes. Stop cooling at 39.5°C.

supplements.name: Electral / ORS Oral Rehydration Salts
supplements.benefit: Fever increases fluid loss. ORS prevents dehydration during fever and supports recovery.
supplements.dose: Offer freely as drinking water during fever. Small amounts frequently.

safety_warnings: NEVER give paracetamol — causes fatal liver failure in dogs even at one tablet
NEVER give ibuprofen — causes fatal kidney failure in dogs
Temperature above 41°C = emergency vet immediately
Fever in unvaccinated or tick-exposed dogs = test for Ehrlichiosis and Leptospirosis

cause: Bite from another animal (most common), sharp object contact, road rash from fall, or post-surgical wound. Dog bite wounds in particular are deceptive — the skin hole looks small but the puncture goes deep and is heavily contaminated with bacteria.
warning: Dog bite wounds ALWAYS need antibiotic treatment even if they look small — the puncture is deep and dog mouths contain Pasteurella bacteria causing severe infections within 24-48 hours.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate (Clavamox)
medicines.type: Antibiotic — First choice for bite wounds
medicines.dose: 12.5-25 mg/kg twice daily for 7-10 days
medicines.note: Covers Pasteurella and Staphylococcus — the bacteria in dog bites. Always prescribed for bite wounds.

topical_treatments.name: Betadine (Povidone-Iodine 1%) Wound Wash
topical_treatments.active: Povidone-Iodine diluted to weak tea color
topical_treatments.use: Flush the wound thoroughly with diluted Betadine immediately after injury. Do NOT use full-strength — dilute with water to weak tea color.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream
topical_treatments.use: Apply thin layer to clean wound after flushing. Promotes healing and prevents maggot infestation in open wounds.

supplements.name: Vitamin C (Wound Healing Support)
supplements.benefit: Essential for collagen synthesis — the protein that repairs skin and tissue. Significantly speeds wound healing.
supplements.dose: 125 mg per 5 kg twice daily during active wound healing

safety_warnings: Dog bite wounds always need antibiotics — even small holes are deeply contaminated
Use E-collar immediately to prevent licking — licking a wound causes severe infection
Wound with maggots (myiasis) = emergency vet. Do NOT remove maggots at home.
Deep wounds, wounds near eyes or joints, or wounds that will not stop bleeding = emergency vet

cause: Canine Cognitive Dysfunction Syndrome (CDDS) causing confusion especially in the evening and night — similar to sundowning in human Alzheimer's patients. Affects dogs over 10 years old. Also caused by pain (arthritis), hearing or vision loss, or vestibular disease (sudden onset).
warning: Sudden onset confusion in a dog that was fine yesterday = vestibular disease or stroke — not just dementia. Vestibular disease improves within 72 hours. If no improvement in 72 hours — see vet urgently.

medicines.name: Selegiline (Anipryl)
medicines.dose: 0.5-1 mg/kg once daily in the morning
medicines.note: Only FDA-approved drug for canine cognitive dysfunction. Takes 4-6 weeks for effect. Helps daytime alertness and night confusion.

supplements.name: MCT Oil (Brain Fuel)
supplements.benefit: Provides alternative ketone fuel for aging neurons. Studies show significant reduction in nighttime confusion and disorientation.
supplements.dose: Start 0.5 ml/kg daily, increase over 2-4 weeks. Too much too fast causes diarrhea.

supplements.name: SAMe (Denosyl) + Omega-3 DHA (Neuroprotective)
supplements.benefit: SAMe supports brain neurotransmitter production. DHA maintains brain cell membrane integrity. Both slow cognitive decline.
supplements.dose: SAMe on empty stomach. Omega-3 with food. Both daily long-term.

safety_warnings: Sudden confusion appearing overnight = vestibular disease or stroke, not just dementia. See vet.
Rule out pain before assuming confusion is cognitive — arthritic dogs in pain pace and appear confused
Keep consistent daily routine — schedule changes significantly worsen cognitive dysfunction
Create a safe sleeping space with no stairs access — confused dogs fall and injure themselves at night

cause: Flies (Lucilia sericata and others) lay eggs in moist wounds, soiled fur, or skin folds. Eggs hatch into maggots within 12-24 hours. The maggots feed on living tissue, releasing toxins that cause rapid tissue destruction and can reach vital organs within days. More common in hot humid weather.
warning: Maggot wounds progress to life-threatening tissue destruction within 24-48 hours. This is a MEDICAL EMERGENCY. Do NOT remove maggots at home — they can burrow deeper when disturbed. Rush to vet immediately.

medicines.name: Ivermectin (Kills maggots — Vet administered)
medicines.dose: 0.2-0.4 mg/kg SC by vet. Kills remaining maggots after mechanical removal.
medicines.note: Do NOT use Ivermectin in Collies, Shelties, Australian Shepherds — MDR1 gene causes fatal toxicity.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate IV + Wound Management
medicines.dose: IV antibiotics at hospital during wound debridement
medicines.note: Maggot wounds are severely infected and require IV antibiotics, surgical debridement, and hospitalization.

topical_treatments.name: Himalaya Scavon Vet Cream (Post-treatment prevention)
topical_treatments.use: Apply to healed wound edges after vet treatment to repel further fly strike. Also apply to any open wound immediately after cleaning to prevent initial fly strike.

supplements.name: Vitamin C + Zinc (Tissue Regeneration)
supplements.benefit: Critical for collagen synthesis and tissue repair after the massive tissue destruction from maggot infestation
supplements.dose: High dose under vet guidance during recovery. Vitamin C 125 mg per 5 kg twice daily.

safety_warnings: EMERGENCY — rush to vet immediately. Do NOT remove maggots at home — they burrow deeper.
Do NOT pour turpentine, kerosene, or salt into the wound — causes additional severe tissue damage
Check ALL wounds, skin folds, and matted fur daily in monsoon and summer — flies strike within hours
After any wound, apply Scavon cream and use E-collar — wet wounds attract flies within minutes in hot weather
